mary-budden-estate.displayName: Mary Budden Estate | Luxury Resorts in Binsar, Uttarakhand

reviews.text:
Our stay at Mary Budden Estate, managed by MRS Collection, was nothing short of magical. This beautiful six-bedroom cottage hotel nestled within the Binsar Wildlife Sanctuary truly lets you immerse yourself in nature and tranquility. Surrounded by forests, birds, and wildlife, it’s the perfect blend of luxury and wilderness.

The service is exceptional — the staff are extremely well-trained, warm, and courteous, always going the extra mile to make you feel comfortable. We celebrated our anniversary here, and they made it so special — baked a delicious cake and organised a candlelight dinner which was beyond what we could have imagined in such a remote setting.

The food was outstanding — easily some of the best we’ve had in a long time. Every meal was thoughtfully prepared and beautifully presented. The cottages are clean, warm, and charming, with a rustic yet elegant feel that perfectly complements the serene surroundings.

We loved the trail walks, the mesmerising views and sunsets, the calmness, and the overall quiet that makes Mary Budden Estate so unique. Their friendly dogs add a lovely homely touch, and the vintage bonfires in the evenings make the experience even cosier. There’s also a good supply of hot water, which is much appreciated given how cold it gets in Binsar.

The only small downside is the approach road, which is a bit rough — but once you arrive, the beauty and peace of the estate make it absolutely worth it.

Highly recommended for anyone looking for a luxurious yet soulful getaway from city life — a place where time slows down, and nature embraces you in every way. 🌿🔥🐾
reviews.author: Tamanna Bakshi

reviews.text: What a place it is. Our 2 nights and 3 days stay has been amazing. The ambience, food, staff, location everything was excellent
reviews.author: Srinivas Pasupuleti

reviews.text: If I could give 10 stars, I would. This is a breathtakingly beautiful place. But the service, food and the staff's utter kindness will move you more. Special mention for Vijay and Ankit. They really were exceptional in taking care of things and making the stay as warm and comfortable as possible
reviews.author: Zarana Mithani

reviews.text:
Mary Budden Estate in Binsar is a true gem! The scenic drive through the Binsar Wildlife Sanctuary is an experience in itself, and the estate's location is simply breathtaking. I'd give it a perfect 10 out of 10 for its stunning surroundings.

We had the opportunity to stay at True Indigo Lodge, where we spent 2 unforgettable nights. The Lodge offered stunning views, and the highlight of our stay was Ramsay's walk - a 5 hour trek through the wildlife sanctuary with a packed breakfast. It was an exhilarating experience that we'll cherish forever.

The food was delicious, and the staff were courteous and attentive. What truly made our stay special was the estate manager, Sunanda Pant, who went above and beyond to make us feel welcome. They even arranged a surprise cake for my birthday and managed to get Bal Mithai from Almora, which was a lovely touch.

Despite being a bit far from our place, we'd love to visit again. Marry Budden Estate has left a lasting impression on us, and we left a piece of our hearts behind. If you're looking for a serene and picturesque getaway, this estate is a must visit.
reviews.author: Anirudh Singh

reviews.text:
Nestled in the lap of nature, this property is a serene escape that soothes the soul with its beauty—but what truly made our stay unforgettable was the incredible warmth and care we received from Mr. Vijay. His kindness, gentleness, and genuine spirit of service touched us deeply. Every interaction felt personal, every moment filled with thoughtfulness and love. It was as if we were being cared for by family. The entire staff radiates that same warmth—gracious, attentive, and always ready with a smile. We felt so at home, so held, and so cared for. This isn’t just a place to stay—it’s a place that fills your heart.

Also, a heartfelt thank you to the chef, who went out of his way to curate meals around my preferences
reviews.author: Dr. Kkity S Sancheti

sarai-at-toria.editorialSummary: Casual all-inclusive ecolodge set along a river, featuring organic meals, a bakery & tiger safaris.

reviews.text: We stayed 2 nights and should have stayed longer.  Really enjoyable, good food, great location.  Only snag was that our agency hadn't warned us to book a safari beforehand, so we missed out - Joanna said there's a >90% chance of seeing tigers, and other guests all saw them.  But we went on the river twice  - very calm and peaceful.  Thoroughly recommend it.
reviews.author: Tom Richardson

reviews.text: Truly spectacular place Raghu and Joanna have put together here. We had a fantastic naturalist, Ruthi, who came with us on two safaris and a gorgeous morning boat ride. He was a lovely guy and so knowledgeable. We got lucky with our tiger spotting too. The manager Piyush (sp.?) and his staff were fantastic and the service and food were through the roof. They also had a very gifted pastry chef!!
reviews.author: Darragh Howard

reviews.text: All staff were helpful. The owners very friendly and informative.  Food was excellent.  Only gave four star for location as a bit of a drive to park gate but no real problem.
reviews.author: martin doughty

reviews.text: The location is fantastic, set on the banks of the river Ken. You can watch river right from the property. Property follows on eco friendly practices helping for a greener space. Rooms are spacious and very relaxing with private sit out with each cottage. Rooms do not have AC but ceiling fans are enough to cool, we went during the first week of this April 2024 with 3 families and kids and we didn't feel the need of any AC. Winters are ofcourse chilly and very pleasant to stay and experience the place. Boat Ride during the stay was the highlight of the stay. Must try. Food is really superb, great cook. All freshly prepared from their in-house farm, recipes are interesting, simple and tasty. Staff is friendly and polite in serving you with a smile. Piyush, the incharge at the property took good care of us like home. Listened to our every need attention to detail especially for all our kids demand. He is a very gentle guy all the time smiling. The property is set tastefully by their owners very meticulously. Don't forget to watch the founders wildlife documentary at the property before you plan to go for any safari, then one can connect really well with the place, jungle and the fauna. If you are accompanying kids, make sure to carry their enough past time games, activities for them as there is not much to explore for kids at the property.
reviews.author: Sanjeev Robinson Paul

reviews.text:
My stay at Sarai at Toria was nothing short of transformative. Its an experience that nurtures your soul and teaches you so much about life, nature, and the art of slowing down.

From the moment I arrived, Raghu and Joanna welcomed me with such warmth and genuine kindness that it felt like I was being embraced by old friends. They are nothing less than angels, creating a haven where every corner reflects their passion for sustainability and harmony with nature.

The stunning riverside setting, the thoughtfully designed cottages, and the farm-to-table meals made with love all contribute to an atmosphere that encourages introspection and connection. It’s the kind of place where you not only rest but also reset—physically, emotionally, and mentally.

Sarai at Toria leaves a profound impact. You don’t just leave with memories, you leave as a different person, carrying a piece of its magic within you. Thank you, Raghu and Joanna, for creating a space that feels like a sanctuary for the soul. I can’t wait to return.
reviews.author: Lachic Rigzin

reviews.text:
We had the most incredible stay at Bera Safari Lodge and honestly couldn’t recommend it more highly. From the moment we arrived, the owners and staff made us feel so welcome and genuinely wanted us to have the best experience possible. The accommodation was beautiful, with thoughtfully designed rooms and every detail carefully considered. The food was absolutely delicious and incredibly generous, and evenings spent around the campfire with a drink and conversation before outdoor buffet dinners were such a special part of the experience.
The safaris were outstanding. We went out twice daily with a knowledgeable guide and driver, visiting different locations and learning about a wide variety of wildlife and ecosystems. Over our stay we were lucky enough to see leopards, sloth bears, countless bird species, antelope, foxes, hogs and more. The guides spoke excellent English, were passionate and informative, and happily answered all our questions. We also appreciated the thoughtful touches like binoculars in the vehicles, and the guides taking incredible wildlife photos for us so we could simply stay present and enjoy the experience.
What really stood out was the respect shown toward the animals and the environment. The team clearly prioritises ethical wildlife viewing and conservation, allowing animals to simply be observed naturally without disturbance or pressure. It made the whole experience feel authentic, respectful and deeply memorable. The owner’s passion and expertise as a wildlife photographer also added another level to the experience. Combined with the beautiful setting, swimming pool, amazing hospitality and unforgettable safaris, this was truly one of the highlights of our trip to India.
reviews.author: Mel

reviews.text:
Our stay at Bera Safari Lodge was fantastic. The lodge has such a welcoming feel while still delivering a truly authentic safari experience.

The guides — and the owner, Shatrunjay — are the real deal: passionate conservationists and genuine advocates for the land and wildlife. Their deep knowledge and respect for the animals and ecosystem makes every safari feel meaningful, not just exciting. Seeing leopards in their natural habitat with people who truly care about protecting them was unforgettable.

After a day in the bush, winding down with a beautifully cooked meal at the lodge was the perfect end to each day. Wonderful hospitality, professional service, and an experience that feels like a true safari.
reviews.author: Charlie Easton

reviews.text: This is much more like a home stay and so really lovely as you get to know the staff and the other guests. Rooms were lovely. Safaris with extremely knowledgeable naturalists and drivers. Everyone very kind to our three children. Food eaten together around one table is a set meal but you can always ask for something else in advance. They did everything they could to accommodate our fussy children!
reviews.author: Hank Shep

reviews.text:
What a fantastic experience it was for us to stay at Bera Safari Lodge. Everything here was just perfect.
The hosts, Shatrunjay and Katyayani, were exceptional. You can truly see how much heart they’ve poured into creating this space, especially for people who genuinely love watching wildlife.

The staff deserves a special mention, well trained, great attention to detail, and extremely caring. You feel looked after at every step.

I’ve travelled across India for work, and this is honestly the first time I’ve stayed in a room with two washrooms. And that’s luxury. When your day starts at 5 AM, these small thoughtful things make life so much easier.

The food was great as well. Simple, comforting, and done right.
A place that understands wildlife, hospitality, and people. Would love to return.
reviews.author: Panchami Gaitonde

reviews.text:
Had a really great stay at Bera Safari Lodge. Proper special place. The guides were brilliant, huge shout out to Mehal for his kind and thoughtful insights and CV for his huge knowledge of the area and wildlife, they made the whole experience. We saw three leopards and the jeep drives were a lot of fun racing around through the landscape and spotting things as we went

The setting itself is incredible with the volcanic rock formations and vegetation giving it a really unique feel unlike anywhere else I have been. What made it was getting a glimpse into the local life too, seeing the shepherd communities and how they live alongside the wildlife added a whole extra layer to it

Would absolutely love to come back
reviews.author: Jack Landers

reviews.text:
An unforgettable experience in the heart of Ladakh
We stayed at the Lodge for 5 days.
It is located in a wonderfull place with a great view
The Lodge offers the perfect blend of rustic Himalayan charm and essential comfort. The rooms are well heated and the hospitality is second to none.
A special mention goes to the kitchen staff, every night a  different cuisine of the highest quality
The highlight of our stay was the tracking. The local guides and spotters are world-class.Their ability to spott wildlife from miles awayn is truly mind- blowing
They are equipped with top- tier Swarovski binoculars, which made a huge difference in spotting wildlife at great distances with incredible clarity.
As a photography enthusiast, I also deeply appreciated that they provided professionals tripods and were incredibly helpful in setting up shots,
Thanks to their expertise and dedication we were very lucky to have several incredible sightings: 3 snow leopards, urials, ibex and wolves
If you are looking for an authentic, professional and successful snow leopard expedition this is the right place.
Highly recommended
reviews.author: Lucia Vullo

reviews.text: Absolutely perfect lodge with a top staff, good food and a wonderful hospetility. The spoters, the guide and the driver did an amazing job! Great experience!
reviews.author: Cornelia Dürst

reviews.text: The Snow Leoaprd Lodge and its team is exemplary , from the beauty and comfortably of the lodge ,the perfect advise and coordination, the amazing spotters , the fabulous food and drinks,  viewing wild life and experiencing watching the snow leopard while sipping Khava tea was luxurious indeed !! Would definitely come back for another stay !! Rupal and myself highly recommend this unique property !!
reviews.author: alok patel

reviews.text: Fantastic place! The guides and spotters really work hard to make sure you get the best experience. While you get the opportunity to help support the community. Food was great, guides were great, lodging was great and all the animals were great. Not just the snow leopard.
reviews.author: Lisa Dionne

reviews.text:
Stayed 3 nights
Staff is out of the world and made the stay absolutely worth every penny
Tom the naturalist
Tanzin ..day staff
All the spotters
The two ladies serving
The other helps don't remember the names
But it's definately a must go to
Care for food
Care for animal spotting
Cleanliness
Attention to all.small and big details was phenomenal
I will recommend to all
reviews.author: Amit Bhusari

reviews.text:
I spent five magical days here for the Hiroshige Kato’s Intensive (though it felt far longer in the best way) and it was hands down the most wonderful time with everyone.

The accommodation was amazing, cozy and thoughtfully designed. The food was outstanding and every meal felt like a treat. Special shoutout to hosts Karthik and Nikita for making the whole experience unforgettable with their warmth and attention to detail. Your hospitality blew me away, nothing was overlooked and everything felt utterly seamless from start to finish.

The pottery studio is well organized everything is accessible and you can spend hours and hours there lost in creativity. Some of my best memories include the morning treks, special trip to the lake and bonfires at night.

The farm itself is the most peaceful oasis, everything just seemed calm and perfectly organised. I didn’t feel the lack of anything, surrounded by the best people and fellow students.

Highly recommend for anyone seeking tranquility, creativity, and genuine connection. Hope to come back someday!
reviews.author: Suroor Fatima

reviews.text:
I’m currently staying at Velanga Orchard and Slow Pottery and participating in their intensive pottery program, and the experience has been nothing short of exceptional.

I registered for three consecutive intensives and have completed two so far — one led by Shivu Mahesh and the other by Hiroshige Kato from Seto, Japan. Both workshops were conducted at an extremely high professional level. The sessions included clear explanations, live demonstrations, and generous hands-on practice time. What truly stands out is the accessibility of the masters — both teachers were fully present, available for questions, and deeply committed to each participant’s learning process.

The organization as a whole is outstanding. The studio facilities are excellent, well-equipped, and thoughtfully managed. The accommodation is beautiful and comfortable — charming cottages with all necessary amenities, seamlessly blending into the natural surroundings.

The location itself is magical: an off-the-grid farm set within a large mango orchard, surrounded by nature. It offers a rare opportunity to disconnect and fully immerse yourself in creativity.

The hospitality is remarkable. The hosts are incredibly generous, and beyond studio hours, they thoughtfully organize additional activities that enrich the entire stay. The food is wonderful, the staff is warm, welcoming, and always eager to assist, and the team is clearly experienced in hosting international guests.

This is truly a haven for potters — beginners and professionals, young and older alike. The entire experience is deeply positive, inspiring, and impactful. I wholeheartedly recommend it to anyone serious about ceramics or seeking a meaningful creative retreat.
reviews.author: Yael Kfir

reviews.text:
I had the most beautiful month at Velanga Orchard x Slow Pottery! The space that Karthik and Nikita have created is pure magic…surrounded by mango trees,hills, and the sound of birds all day.

The pottery course is thoughtfully structured by Nikita, whose demonstrations are super detailed and clear. She’s an incredible teacher who is patient, grounded, and truly passionate about the craft.

And then there’s Karthik, the man behind it all. From managing logistics to hosting, he ensures everyone feels safe and has the best time. He goes above and beyond to make everyone feel comfortable.. not just the students, but also the staff and even the wildlife around the farm. A genuine giver in every sense.

From long studio hours to quiet mornings, mountain hikes, bonfires by the lake, delicious food cooked by Padam and Kavita and the sweetest dogs... every day felt grounding and meaningful. It’s a place that slows you down in the best way and reminds you what it means to create with intention.

Can’t recommend it enough for anyone looking to learn pottery while reconnecting with nature ♥️
reviews.author: Shamitha Shetty

reviews.text:
Last month I attended two intensives at Slow Pottery.
My expectations were to simply learn pottery and have a pleasant workshop experience. What I wasn’t prepared for was how completely immersive and special it would be.
Set in a serene mango orchard, the atmosphere is peaceful, calm, and deeply restorative. From morning to night, the days flowed beautifully — learning, creating, laughing, and yet feeling incredibly relaxed inside.
Early morning treks, learning from experts, evenings around the bonfire under the stars, delicious nourishing food throughout the day and company of fellow potters from different parts of the world - this was my life for 18 days. I could not have asked for more!!
The warmth and hospitality of the hosts and staff made it even more special. I left not just with new pottery skills, but with a wonderful sense of joy, calm, and meaningful connections with people from different countries. Truly a memorable experience.
Thank you Nikita and Karthik for this experience,  you guys are truly special and your passion for what you do makes Slowpottery a magical place.
I will definitely be back.
reviews.author: Toral Mehta

reviews.text:
I stayed at Velanga orchard for 10 days for an Obvara firing course with Meenakshi ma’am and honestly, it was such a wholesome experience. The place is super calm and peaceful, exactly what you need to slow down and just be.

The cottages were really nice.. clean, cozy, and so thoughtfully done. You feel comfortable the min you get there. Nikita and Karthik are the sweetest hosts, they make you feel so at home and take care of everything so effortlessly. They even took us to the lake one evening, we had a bonfire and went kayaking which was such a fun and memorable break from the studio.

And the food… I don’t even know where to start. Simple home cooked meals but SOOO good. I’m pretty sure I came back a little heavier because I couldn’t stop eating 😄
Every meal was something to look forward to.

The course itself was amazing, I learned so much in those 10 days. Overall, it didn’t feel like just an intensive, it felt like a little reset. Would 100% recommend if you’re looking to learn pottery and also just a breather from regular life.
reviews.author: jeenal d

reviews.text:
Here’s a more grounded version:

Some experiences are hard to put into words, and Parijaat is one of them. It’s slow, peaceful and genuinely beautiful. The location, the views, the pools — everything is just right.
A warm, hearty breakfast sets the tone for unhurried mornings that somehow melt the stress right out of you.
Kavita and Manoj are wonderful hosts — caring, attentive, and thoughtful in ways that make you feel truly looked after. Every corner of this home has been tended to with love and intention.
If you’re looking for a place that feels both magical and deeply restorative, Parijaat is it. Absolutely flawless — don’t think twice.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
reviews.author: Prachi Sharma

reviews.text:
Staying at Parijaat felt nothing short of magical. This family-owned homestay is truly a home away from home - the kind of place that settles into your heart long after you’ve left. Every moment spent here felt cherished, wrapped in comfort, warmth, and an unspoken sense of belonging.

The hosts were extraordinary gracious, attentive, and genuinely kind. They didn’t just open their doors; they opened their world, creating an atmosphere where you feel welcomed, cared for, and effortlessly at ease. There is a rare sincerity in the way they host, and it transforms the entire experience.

The surroundings radiate peace, and Parijaat has become my personal haven of solace….a place where time slows, where the tiniest details feel thoughtful, and where the beauty of simple living shines through. It’s a space I will always hold close to my heart, and one I will forever look forward to returning to.

Parijaat isn’t just a stay, it’s a feeling. And it’s unforgettable. 🌿✨
~ PrastishK🌸
reviews.author: Prastish Singha

reviews.text:
Exceptional Hospitality and Comfort!
Parijaat Goa exceeded all our expectations. The hosts Kavita and Manoj are incredibly kind and attentive ensuring our stay was perfect and comfortable. The rooms were spotless, beautifully decorated and cozy. Vibrant aesthetic pieces and antique furniture adds such a classic touch to the place.Each morning, the breakfast was fresh, delicious and made with so much care. The location is peaceful, quiet and perfect for unwinding, yet in close proximity to all the happening good places. Our family favourite,
Parijaat Goa is a home away from home.

With Gratitude from Nairs.
reviews.author: S S

reviews.text: Wow! What a lovely setting! A manicured garden that we enjoyed for a whole day. Hospitality and care of our hosts are unmatched. Breakfast as you would like to have it. Made and served with love and a smile that will always be remembered. Great coffee  to get you going for the day! I could go on and on with compliments! Our hosts are new friends now!…. Amie
reviews.author: Amie Singh

reviews.text: The house Kavita and Manoj have built is nothing short of surreal. It takes you to a moment where things get slow and meaningful. The greenery around, the peacocks, the traditional architecture, all of these will tug at your heart if you are looking for a place where you can relax and slow down. What makes it even better are the personalized recommendations from Kavita and Manoj, and their warm hospitality. Absolutely loved being there!
reviews.author: Tanvi Johri

reviews.text: Beautiful heritage property turned into a magical home stay. The owner himself takes keen interest in the day to day running,which makes you feel very welcome. Caring service and excellent food.
reviews.author: Ramakrishnan Rajaram

reviews.text:
Amazing host, thanks to neetuji and his husband and son for taking good care of us. They were always there to help us out, showed around their haveli. We also visited their private reserve with lake and temple.
The rooms are spacious and huge, all amenities are available.
reviews.author: CA Apeksha Mahto

reviews.text:
Had a one night stay, wished we spent more time. Wonderful ambience,  warm and smiling staff. Personal attention by the owner couple added to the delight. Each room has a different splendor,  the food was delicious. Reminded me of my army days.
I believe their farm stay is equally good.
reviews.author: Jitendra Gokhale

reviews.text: A beautiful and wonderful heritage home stay. Mata na madh, lakhpat, bhuj and many other interesting sites are within 2 hour drive. A truly unique experience in a heritage home with very warm, welcoming and caring hosts. Thank you Nituji, Krutarthji and lovely Padmini for such unique and lovely experience❤️
reviews.author: Jigna vyas

reviews.text:
Stayed at Devpur Homestay for 2 nights. Had visited Kutch for a family wedding. But the itinerary suggested by Mr Krutarthsinh not only helped me and my wife to attend the wedding, but also explored nearby places to a great extent. The driver, Mr Juvansinh is a very jovial person who is always ready to help or take you wherever you want, with a smile.  The property is very well maintained, you get a feeling of ethnic surroundings and a comfort of home. Gulabi Medi Room was very spacious and clean. They served hot and yummy breakfast and dinner. The hospitality provided by Mr Krutarthsinh's family was heartening.

Will be looking forward to stay here again if I visit Kutch next time.
reviews.author: Mahesh Dholiya

reviews.text:
Me and my friend had amazing days at Laalee! Laalee is gorgeous and art is felt and lived in every detail.
The rooms are special and we can feel it. We have came to India to study Yoga and to explore the culture and being in Laalee brought us the opportunity to experience the Indian Art, the Krishna Love, the friendly, kindness and love from Indian People and culture.
The food was amazing and unique!
The workers are so kind and ready to help with anything we might need.
They also offer workshops and it is really special. We had the opportunity of doing the art workshop with Shan Bhatnagar and he was so kind and lovely with us. I’m so grateful for my days at Laalee and for each person that made it possible.
I’m going back home with beautiful memories in my heart.
reviews.author: isadora costa

reviews.text:
We had the most amazing stay at Laalee Guest House in Jaipur. It was such an authentic and special experience from start to finish. Richa and Amar were so hospitable, made us feel at home the moment we arrived.

The guesthouse is beautifully and thoughtfully decorated, full of character and traditional charm. Every detail feels authentic and carefully curated, which made our stay even more memorable.

The homemade breakfast was delicious.. everything was homemade. It is one of our highlights of our trip in India ! Thank you Richa!
reviews.author: Eleanor Reynolds

reviews.text:
The Laalee is a beautiful, design-forward oasis on the hustle & bustle of Jaipur. It is impeccably decorated by the artist partner  with each of the nine rooms showcasing a distinct theme/personality. I stayed in the Basant room and my parents stayed in the Mayur room.

The menu for breakfast (we didn’t have lunch or dinner there) was lovingly curated by the chef partner. Everything about the property is tailored to your comfort with all the modern amenities of WiFi, AC, etc. But honestly, you’re better off bringing a good book and lounging outside in the garden or in the lush communal spaces. You can also indulge in a craft coffee with Dimitri’s (visiting Frenchman) Layala Coffee. Really great mini cafe with yummy Indian roasted coffees.

It’s a bit further out from the Pink City and ~40 mins to the Amber Fort & Palace but very manageable with an Uber.

Special thank you to Richa and Amar and their team for a lovely stay.
reviews.author: Nina M

reviews.text:
Where to begin with Laalee? From the moment you walk in, it's like you've entered an oasis in Jaipur. The building is as beautiful, if not more, than the photos show, and while the rooms are on the smaller end, they are so thoughtfully designed and picturesque. Each room in the building has its own charm and character.
Staff are incredibly attentive and thoughtful - on multiple occasions, they offered to walk me somewhere in the neighborhood. They always asked if you were doing okay and made immediate efforts to ensure your comfort.
The food was excellent and reasonably priced. Nothing was too heavy and most everything was an interesting take on classic dishes.
My only regret is that we did not stay longer. I will absolutely be coming back next time we're in Jaipur!
reviews.author: Gavin Crowell-Williamson

reviews.text: This boutique hotel is an absolute GEM!  The rooms, the service and the FOOD, my gosh the FOOD was astounding!  It’s located in a quiet neighborhood and and it gets 5++ stars from me!!
reviews.author: Brenda Sedberry

reviews.text:
This is a hidden gem we found in Tamil Nadu. We took a overnight train from Chennai to Pollachi and then a taxi to this place.
It doesn't have more than 4-5 rooms so its utterly peaceful. The rooms were spacious yet cozy and the bathroom was a highlight in itself, unimaginably big, with a separate powder room.
The entire premise is a colonial bungalow with antiques everywhere and fireplaces.

The staff is very welcoming and congenial.

The food needs special mention as it was one of the best culinary experiences I ever had. We stayed for 2 days and every time (lunch, dinner & breakfast) there was a different menu and different cutlery too. The freshness of the food and the delight with which it was served made the entire experience quite unforgettable. I can return for food alone.

There are 3 playful cats in the premise so my daughter was fully engaged. The sunsets were magical to say the least.
The place also offers paid activities like hikes around the property, jeep safari, picnic by the river which I think should not be missed.

We visited in December, so the weather was absolutely perfect. The colors look more vibrant than usual and ohh the flowers were just so pretty. I think I am going to have a family tradition where we spend a weekend here every winter.
reviews.author: bushra maimuna

reviews.text: We stayed at Sinnadorais Sullivan Bungalow last week for a few days. Scenic surroundings and the weather were awesome. The staff Kalai, Joel and the entire staff  ably guided by Surabhi were most helpful , courteous and made our stay very comfortable. The food was outstanding with the carefully curated menu in which there was a very wide spread of delicious  food. Overall a very relaxing and enjoyable time. 🤩
reviews.author: Ramkumar L

reviews.text:
We were contemplating where to visit for a week's break with summer already in. We wanted a calm , peaceful, cool location and took a chance of planning a trip down south expecting it to be hotter than Pune.
When we started from Coimbatore towards Sinna Dorai's upper paralai estate, it was hot as expected but as soon as we started moving up the Valparai hills the air started to get cooler and the view started to get nicer. An hour's ride through multiple hairpin turns you reach this 100+ year old british Bungalow at the top nested with the Anamalai forest reserve on one side and Tea plantation all around it. We were pleasantly surprised by how cool and pleasant the weather was here and the general quiteness around. The greenery around was soothing for sore eyes while the sound of bird's chirping was soothing for our ears.
The bungalow has retained its old world charm while providing for new world luxury. The rooms are big , spacious and neatly maintained. The staff Mr Joel, Mr Murugan and Ms kalai are very kind and always there for you. The food served is exceptionally good.
You can go on nature walks, do birding, take a bath in nearby stream or literally laze around and do nothing. If you are lucky you may spot a leopard. We saw a Biason, barking deer, and many birds.
In summary if you are looking for a peaceful and quiet stay within nature away from the rush of city life , add this to your list of places to visit. The season doesn't matter as it's a hill station all year round.
reviews.author: Divya Mulmule

reviews.text:
What an amazing experience, flawlessly catered to by a dedicated and quality oriented service by the staff of Sinna Dorai; Kalai, Murugan, chef Ashirad, Joel and Alwin  and managed expertly by Surabhi!

The rooms were managed so well, the property : in one with nature, and the food was outstanding!!!

The knowledge of the staff regarding the ecosystem of Valparai and the care they exhibit towards it, is something we can all aspire to.

One of the best experiences of this decade 🌟
reviews.author: Viv

reviews.text: I was on a very short stay at Upper Paralai location with my family from Mar 21-23. It has been a pleasant memorable experience. The location is very good if you want to get away from the city noises and just be there for to enjoy nature & scenery. The staff makes it more special and in our case thanks to Joel, Kalai & kitchen staff for the sumptuous meals. Special mention to Ms.Kalai who curates the menu and prepared meals to suit our dietary choices and restrictions. Great experience and looking forward to the next visit
reviews.author: Prasanna Venkatesh Srinivasan

glenburn-tea-estate.editorialSummary: Refined, Colonial-style hotel on a 19th-century tea estate with quaint quarters & mountain views.

reviews.text:
Bit of a challenge to get to Glenburn from Bagdogra Airport, but idyllic once you get there.  We stopped for a little picnic on our way  (it’s about a three hour drive), and had a pit stop at Margaret’s Hope, very clean services! Set in the mountains, about 33 km from the town of Darjeeling, it’s an oasis of peace and quiet and ideal for some relaxation and introspection.

Andrew (the manager) and his team made our stay as comfortable and carefree as any 5-star hotel we have stayed at in our travels. The food was filling and delicious. The rooms are spacious and very comfortable (we loved the electric blanket on our bed to warm our feet at bedtime!! ). Bonfires before dinner presented a great opportunity to meet and get to know other guests. Our excursion to Darjeeling and Mall Road was memorable and costly!!!  My wife bought a beautiful cashmere shawl in one of the shops - a high quality, silky smooth souvenir of Darjeeling!!

From a tea estate tour and tea tasting to hikes along the river (to Sikkim, which we were not able to enter since we didn’t have the requisite permit), to a waterfall hike and lunch in an orchard, there is no shortage of activities to choose from that will work up a hearty appetite for the next meal.

We had a wonderful stay, a great opportunity to meet and make news friends!!
reviews.author: Fred Leitner

reviews.text:
I reached there after being picked up from the airport
It's a small place, not the way it looked in the website and photos. It's not doubt look fairy tale but it's has it flaws and in that the pricing is not correct.
The rooms are not temperature controlled. So one has to keep on wearing wollens. Room heater is too small for the room
Room is beautiful and spacious but the bathroom basin is cracked and so is the bathtub.
Hot water doesn't come in the shower properly and one has to wait a lot wasteing to keep the running water for the hot water in the basin.
Tea cosy has tea stains all over and the crockery is chipped of paints, look old. Golden rim of the cups are gone and so is the cutlery, tarnished.
It's not the cost of the money for the hotel. Their is no doubt old world charm is there. But not worth a visit.
reviews.author: Cuttocut Entertainment

reviews.text: Just visited Glenburn and can’t recommend it enough for anyone looking to escape city life. The property is absolutely stunning, lush, serene, and full of old-world charm. The factory tour and tea tasting were beautifully done. A special mention to Andrew!
reviews.author: Jasleen Gumber

reviews.text:
Had a wonderful week here just before Christmas. The accommodation is fabulous, the food is amazing and the service is very good. I enjoyed some lovely walks from Glenburn with their guides, the scenery is fabulous and all the people in the local area very friendly.
Everything at Glenburn was to a high standard. I also stayed down at the river lodge if your there for more than a couple of nights well worth doing.
The journey to get there is quiet long but well worth the journey when you arrive.
I will return.
reviews.author: mark kempster

reviews.text:
stay in colonial-style cottages with sweeping verandas, explore 1,600 acres of tea gardens, and spend unhurried days on nature walks, riverside picnics, or tea tours with estate workers.

Still run by the Prakash family, Glenburn is as much about tea as it is about slowing down and settling into life in the hills.

Top experiences:

🏡 Night stays at a log cabin by the river
🚶 Walks through tea fields and forest trails
🍲 Tantalising Nepali and Indian dishes
🔥 Evenings with high tea around a bonfire
🧺 Riverside picnics

Spotlighting RARE Touchstones :

🌳 Biodiversity Preservation and Sustainable Farming Practices
🤝 Community Empowerment through Education and Welfare
reviews.author: Bhaba Jyoti Nath

the-lodge-at-wah.editorialSummary: Laid-back guesthouse offering homey rooms, as well as a garden, a lounge & mountain views.

reviews.text:
The experience at The Lodge at Wah was truly mesmerising. Beyond the amazing look & feel of the property, top notch food and hospitality; the stay experience helped me unwind in the true sense.

Located at the foothills overlooking snow capped mountains, views from the lodge and the tea gardens are a sight to behold.

From eating organically grown produce in kansa thaalis (the Himachali Dham) to breathing fresh air, the experience is a perfect combination of simple, sustainability planked yet a luxurious experience.

The next door tea room, tea tours and factory tours provided options to try new experiences rather than always being a couch potato (though the nippy weather warranted it :p)

The pizza day and bonfire nights at the lodge ensured that the evenings were fun filled and eventful.

The hosts Surya and Upasana and Mr. and Mrs. Prakash welcoming and they ensured that we were always taken care of.

The experience makes me yearn to definitely come back again and makes me realise how simple practices can be so fulfilling.

Bir and Dharamshala are an hours drive from the lodge, making it a great place for a 3 night stay with lots of day trip options nearby.

If you're seeing this before making your bookings, would highly recommend this place!

If you've made your bookings, I'd rather you check up the cancellation options and visit The Lodge at Wah!
reviews.author: varun killa

reviews.text:
In such times when you come across a plays that not only meets n exceeds your expectations but blows you away

All that ewas possible because mr Deepak Prakash {owner) hadd a dream to create a space that is modern yet old school, sf sustainable, following n organic way of cooking yet being modern.

The rooms reflect his vision with attention to a simple details.

Balti lota to bath, taala chaabi instead of new age licka, stone floor n bed, no television to name a few

Yet there is sooooo much more u can do.. they give you a chance to connect back to yr roots

Storytelling is a major ingredient that keeps you alive and wanting for more at this place.

From food outdoor to everything comes with a story.

Best part about this ace is a garden whichis
Not manicured but better than
Most u have seen

Place without a library but everywhere you go there is a book that follows u

A kitchen without a menu but food more tastier organic an better variety than most 5 stars.

Icing on the cake is their common area where you come in as a stranger but go back with connections with people you never knew

It's once to stay before you die kind of property

Now the stars who make this sky shine

Saloni- Estate Manager, their is hardly a problem for which she doesn't have a solution.. whatever uask for answer is always a yes... Whether a late breakfast, early check in, places to roam, food to eat...and to top it all she is an excellent storyteller. She is the star that ulluminates this sky

Chef- an expert of cusines both local n global. Try their, dham , tari poha, egg kejriwal and local chutneys ..n to top it all pakora with tea leaves it

Staff- vishal, shubham , badal n Lawrence. Have smile on their lipn and always prompt in service

A place worth coming back for
reviews.author: Rohit Bhargava

reviews.text:
This was the most beautiful accommodation of my five-week trip to India. The drive from Amritsar to the lodge was long, but it was worth it.

The lodge is absolutely stunning, situated right in the middle of a tea plantation. The staff were the nicest I encountered in India. Everyone was incredibly friendly and attentive. I felt very comfortable and welcome from the moment I arrived. The rooms are large and very stylishly furnished. The bathroom is also beautiful. The bed was very comfortable, and they even have heated duvets! I was there in January, and this was the only accommodation in India where I wasn't cold. The rest of the accommodation is fantastic as well. There's a large garden with lots of plants, perfect for relaxing. Every evening there was a bonfire, and the staff would sit with us and chat. So, even as a solo traveler, you don't feel alone.

The food was amazing. There was something different every day, and it always tasted heavenly. The portions are huge.

Next to the lodge are the tea plantations. There are benches there where you can sit and soak up the atmosphere and enjoy life. There's a museum, and you can try about 20 varieties of tea. There's plenty to do in the area, and the lodge helps organize excursions and arranges drivers. While the air is very polluted in many Indian cities, you can breathe easily in Himachal Pradesh. After four days at the lodge, I noticed my lungs had recovered from the smog.

The nearest airport is near Dharamsala, and the transfer went perfectly. My stay at the lodge gave me a different perspective on India, and I'm so glad I had the opportunity to see this part of the country.

A huge thank you to the team for the fantastic time. Anyone who wants to see a tea plantation in India and enjoy a relaxing stay should book Wah Lodge.

Warm regards from Germany to the Kangra Valley
reviews.author: Laura Wloka

reviews.text: We arrived in Palampur from Dharamshala after touring Rajasthan, Uttarakhand and the Punjab. Immediately the athmosphere changed to an environment of peace and harmony where the pace of life is determined by the growth of buds in the tea plantation. Nestled beneath the Dhauladar range, The Lodge gave us access to wonderful hikes in the Himalayan foothills whilst we were adapting our taste in tea to the elite end of the spectrum! My new motto in tea will be ' white like the Himalayas'! Thank you to everyone for your very special hospitality. To fellow travellers: build your India trip around a stay at The Lodge at Wah. It is an unmissable experience.
reviews.author: tim Thurston

reviews.text: The stay was extremely comfortable within a beautiful property surrounded by nature, where we woke up to the calls of birds and a delightful view of the mountain ranges. The food spread had good variety and was quite tasteful. The hosts were warm and friendly and made the experience worthwhile.
reviews.author: Jayshree Killa

khem-villas.editorialSummary: Warm cottages, rooms & tents, plus game drives, a spa & complimentary vegetarian meals.

reviews.text:
We stayed for 2 nights at Khem villas while visiting Ranthambore. The property is very peaceful. The staff is extremely attentive and accommodating. They welcome you with towels, smooth check-in, and give a lot of information about the property and its environment. The stay is with full board, with produce from the property. All the food was delicious and memorable. Some of the best garlic naan I’ve had.

At night there is a campfire with complimentary appetizers, a great way to relive the safari moments of the day. Before you leave for your safari they give you blankets, water and snacks on request.

We stayed in a standard room, and have no complaints. The room was spacious, the bed comfortable and there was a lot of hot water.

We also took the opportunity for a guided nature walk with Dinesh. It was amazing to see how much wildlife is on the property: crocodiles, eagles, herons and footprints of leopards.

I would definitely stay here again.
reviews.author: Cabeth Dekker

reviews.text: Very close to Ranthambore National Forest if you are going for a safari. Transportation - compliments of the hotel available for the safari. Very attentive staff with naturalist on hand to explain the surroundings. Rooms are very comfortable and the included food is organic and delicious. Loved our time there.
reviews.author: NY Cowgirl

reviews.text:
We stayed for 3 nights and had a truly memorable experience. The property is nestled amidst lush greenery, beautifully maintained, and feels very close to nature. Our rooms were welcoming, thoughtfully decorated with fresh flowers on arrival.

The hospitality is outstanding—warm, sincere, and effortless. Food at every meal—breakfast, lunch, and dinner—was prepared using very fresh ingredients, with an excellent spread. Nothing was oily; everything was light, flavourful, and absolutely delicious.

They went the extra mile for our child by customizing non-spicy meals and adding items he enjoyed. Safari coordination was seamless, with timely vehicle pickups, packed snacks, and hot towels on return.

Evenings with bonfire, folk music, stargazing, and nature walks were beautifully curated. Located very close to the forest, Khem Villas is perfect for a luxurious, family-friendly Ranthambore stay. We would love to return.
reviews.author: Hetal Madhvani

reviews.text: Stayed here in a posh tent which isn't really a tent, it's much better, as it has insulated hard walls and it looks good. Tents have heating wifi and excellent service. Food is very good. A particularly good feature is the evening camp fire where guests sit around the fire before dinner for drinks and canapés and meeting the other guests
reviews.author: John Otty

reviews.text:
I highly recommend a stay at Khem Villas. The rooms are beautifully appointed and the setting is tranquil. It is a pleasure to watch the wildlife and they offer a complementary guided walk. The staff are hugely attentive and go out of the way to cater for all your needs. The food is amazing, using produce from their organic garden. When you want a cuppa, they deliver it to your place, which makes it super relaxing and it's all included in the price!
If you are visiting Ranthambore, this is the place to stay.
We had a few issues with our safari trips, even though this was not their responsibility, the general manager went out of his way to help us resolve these.
reviews.author: Martin High

reviews.text:
We had a wonderful stay at Singinawa Jungle Lodge, Kanha. The entire experience was extremely relaxing, enjoyable, and fulfilling.

A big thank you to Mr. Ankit, Ms. Jaskeerat, Mr. Akhilesh, and the entire team. Every staff member, from the servers and waiters to housekeeping and support staff, was very polite, friendly, and always ready to help. They took care of us in every possible way and made us feel completely at home.

The food was excellent throughout our stay. Breakfast, lunch, dinner, and even the packed meals provided during safaris were all very well prepared and delicious.

The rooms were beautifully maintained and cleaned twice a day, which made our stay even more comfortable. We also enjoyed spending time at the swimming pool and taking nature walks around the property with the naturalist.

The activities organized by the lodge, including pottery, Gond art, tribal dance performances, and the museum visit, were all memorable and thoughtfully arranged. They gave us a wonderful opportunity to learn about the local culture and enjoy the beauty of the place.

The biggest highlight of our trip was finally spotting a tiger on our last safari. It fulfilled a wish that had remained unfulfilled for many years and made our trip truly special.

Singinawa is the perfect place for anyone looking for a peaceful jungle getaway. It really feels like a home away from home, with caring people who go out of their way to make your stay comfortable and memorable.

We thoroughly enjoyed our trip and would highly recommend Singinawa Jungle Lodge to anyone visiting Kanha.
reviews.author: Saurabh Sharma

reviews.text:
We had an absolutely wonderful stay at Singinawa Jungle Lodge, Kanha. The accommodation was beautiful, comfortable, and thoughtfully designed, making our stay very relaxing. The food was delicious, and every meal was something we looked forward to.

What truly made our experience special was the excellent service. Every member of the staff was friendly, polite, and always ready to help with a smile. Their hospitality made us feel welcome from the moment we arrived.

The safaris were very well organized and enjoyable, and the naturalists were knowledgeable, passionate, and great companions during our wildlife excursions. They added so much value to our overall experience.

Everything about the resort exceeded our expectations—the stay, the food, the service, the safaris, and the people. Singinawa Jungle Lodge has become a favorite destination for all of us, and we would love to visit again. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a memorable wildlife and nature experience in Kanha!
reviews.author: Shriya Anupam

reviews.text:
Our stay at Singinawa Jungle Lodge
was truly one of the best experiences we’ve had. We stayed for 3 nights, and everything about the property — from the hospitality to the attention to detail — was exceptional. The team made us feel incredibly special throughout our stay and went above and beyond to fulfill every special request with warmth and care.

A special mention to Piyush, who was our point of contact throughout the stay. He is absolutely outstanding at what he does — extremely attentive, kind, and genuinely caring. Every arrangement and every little comfort was handled perfectly by him. He is not only excellent at his work, but also a genuinely kind and humble person who makes every guest feel valued, comfortable, and truly at home.

The nature walks with Suneel and Rohit were another highlight of our trip. Both of them are incredibly knowledgeable and passionate, and exploring the jungle with them was such a magical experience. We learned so much and got beautiful insights into the forest and wildlife.

The entire food and hospitality staff was wonderful, especially Jay and Yadav Ji, who looked after us with great warmth and care. Many thanks to Chef Sunil as well, who personally checked on us after meals and asked about our  preferences for the next one. Such thoughtful hospitality made our stay even more special.

Thank you to the entire Singinawa team for making this stay so special and unforgettable. We already can’t wait to come back again!
reviews.author: Neha Kulkarni

reviews.text:
Our family stayed at Singinawa Jungle Lodge from 24th–26th May 2026, occupying 3 rooms, and the experience was nothing short of exceptional. From the moment we arrived, the warm welcome and hospitality made us feel truly special. The rooms were luxurious, spacious, and beautifully maintained, while the food and drinks throughout our stay were absolutely excellent.

A special mention to Chef Mr. Sunil Rawat for the wonderful culinary experience.

We completed 4 safaris during our stay with naturalist Mr. Rakesh Solanki, fondly known as Kaka Ji, in the lodge’s special bucket-seat safari vehicle. The vehicle was extremely comfortable and spacious for all 6 of us, making every safari relaxed and enjoyable. Kaka Ji was outstanding — highly knowledgeable, thoroughly professional, and incredibly passionate about wildlife. He truly elevated our safari experience.

The housekeeping team was prompt and meticulous, the swimming pool was fantastic, and every aspect of the stay reflected attention to detail and genuine care for guests.

Overall, a solid 10/10 experience. We will definitely return and will gladly recommend Singinawa Jungle Lodge to many more people.
reviews.author: Dr Kartikeya Singh

reviews.text: I was staying here last week with my two daughters. I can't begin to say how wonderful it was. From the staff, who were so professional, friendly and attentive, to the food which was fantastic, to the lodge and bungalows. All spotlessly clean, very well equipped and just excellent. Thank you all for a life-changing experience.
reviews.author: Moyra MacMath

reviews.text: Stayed at this beautiful tea estate tucked away in the forest — it truly feels like being in the middle of nature. The property has little cozy sitting corners everywhere, perfect for reading, sipping tea, or just soaking in the calm. Surrounded by greenery and quiet, it’s peaceful and refreshing, almost like a hidden retreat. Also they make homemade food right in front of you, so you never feel like you are eating outside, If you love nature and a serene atmosphere and home cooked food, this place is perfect. 🌿☕
reviews.author: Amit Jhurawat

reviews.text: Short stay at the estate run by Keshav and his family. Amazing food. Great nature sounds & lots of greenery which is beautiful to sleep and wake up to!
reviews.author: Rashi J (Thatveganyogini)

reviews.text: Warm and lovely stay in the middle of nature. Great host and staff who make you feel really welcomed. The place has recreational activities to do and lots of space to just be. As a tea lover, I had a great time learning all about the tea-making process.
reviews.author: Yashna Bhanushali

reviews.text:
One of the most hospitable and amazing green place. The owners ,Kanoi family is super hosts. Food is all fresh, organic and mostly they get everything farmfresh. Room is equipped with all amenities yet keeping the natural charm alive.

4 hours drive from Guwahati Airport and plan for two days.
reviews.author: Dr.Rama Moondra

reviews.text: Immersive is the word when it comes to experiencing Sapoi Tea Estate. Due to my work, I get to travel extensively and enjoy most amazing places in our incredible country. This is a gem- tranquil and sublime. Keshav is the perfect host. Happy and happening. The temple in the estate is first I have ever seen. The rooms are contained, facilities amazing and the food makes one content. Terrific staff and surrounding. Thank you for hosting us. We will be back again.
reviews.author: Alok Singh

shaam-e-sarhad.editorialSummary: Seasonal, indigenous-run resort offering simple tents & mud huts, along with vegetarian meals.

reviews.text:
We were given 2 of the worst possible tents. We were to stay for 2 nights but checked out within a day. Yes, we knew Rann was cold, but with huge gaps in the tent walls, there was no way one could sleep in these tents.
After some ruckus, the staff came and stuffed bedsheets in the gap, which helped to a certain extent.

The food was good and tasty. The tea for the cold was excellent.
reviews.author: Prathima Acharya

reviews.text: All things are alright only food plan update required,I am stayed twice here nothing bed experience first time food was OK,but now update required
reviews.author: Dharmesh Panchal

reviews.text:
Excellent place to stay. Don’t expect luxury of Tent City, basic but nice.

Staff was welcoming and helped with every requirement.

(Jain) Food was delicious and unlimited.

This place is run by locals of Banni area and hence not commercial.

Evening was program included bonfire and local Sindhi Sufi music.

Breakfast was light but sufficient.

It takes about 15-20 min to reach Rann Utsav from here which was comfortable. Currently it’s two lane road and construction of four lane is in full swing. Should be done in Feb 2025.

All in all, a great place to stay and great experience.
reviews.author: Jeegar Shah

reviews.text: The last week we(self,wife and daughter) spent 3 wonderful days at this resort and had a lifetime experience. The "Bhunga" stay was our first experience and has left life long memories full of pleasure and joy. The necessary amenities were there,comfortable beds,hot water,nice w/c and bathroom,good tasty home like local delicacies but the silver lining was the approach ,behavior and courtesy of attending staff as they looked after every minute requirements, for which we,indeed, are grateful. Our most interaction was with the young lad Rahim bhai and Mushtakq bhai.The incharge Mr Rashid and Siddiq  bhai too were at their best. Firoze Bhatti  who was our companion in trip is an extraordinary cordial person, good human being, a perfect driver and very considerate ,caring and humble person. When visiting next time have "Shame E Sarhad" your abode.
reviews.author: Ashok Bhatia

reviews.text: It's a beautiful resort. Little away from white desert but that's an advantage also. It's away from crowded place. The people there are so warm and hospitality is absolutely amazing. The food is so homely and tasty. I would only suggest the tents shud be little more maintained.
reviews.author: kirti shah

reviews.text:
I can’t praise this place enough — our stay was truly exceptional. The property is absolutely beautiful, surrounded by lush greenery that makes you feel completely immersed in nature. We stayed in the Premium Villa, and I genuinely don’t think our experience could have been any better. It felt incredibly private, almost like we were the only guests on the property. Every element in the room has been thoughtfully designed and carefully chosen, adding to the sense of luxury.
The infinity pool was right next to our room, offering stunning views that we could never get tired of. I also joined the bird-watching walk and the plantation drive — both were wonderfully informative and a lot of fun. The lake walk was another lovely experience.

The food was excellent as well. Breakfast was especially good, and enjoying our meals in the open dining area surrounded by greenery and birdsong was unforgettable. The dosa at breakfast was the best I had during my entire trip.
I truly wish we could have stayed longer. Pepper Trail is an exceptional escape, and I’m definitely coming back!
reviews.author: ridhima ranga

reviews.text: This is fabulous hotel - one of the nicest places I have stayed. The grounds are magical and filled with a diverse array of plants and trees - just magical. The service in the restaurant and the food was first- class. We stayed in the treehouses which were clean and comfortable and delighted in being up in the treetops with the monkeys. The infinity pool is a stunning place to swim and relax. The staff were absolutely fantastic - we thoroughly enjoyed our stay.
reviews.author: sacha cook

reviews.text: Had a good experience. The bunglow over  150 years is a must visit. The staff, Manager, Amal all were so polite and humble - added more comfort to stay. Naturalist Mr. Kishore gave a jeep ride - view of hornbill, 150 year old coffee plant, giant bamboo will last long in memory.  Everything was excellent except the rude behaviour of reservation staff ruining the whole trip.
reviews.author: Varun V Gopal

reviews.text: We stayed at Pepper Trail and had booked the Pool Villa and the Mackenzie Suite. The property offers a very peaceful and serene environment, making it an ideal place to unwind. The Pool Villa is especially perfect for couples, offering privacy and comfort. The staff were exceptionally well trained, courteous, and attentive throughout our stay. The food was very good, with consistent quality across meals. Overall, it was a very pleasant experience and a stay we would highly recommend.
reviews.author: Bertin Remegious Oscar

reviews.text:
"Nestled within a lush 200-acre coffee and spice plantation, Pepper Trail is a true gem in Wayanad. The experience of staying in their luxurious tree houses and heritage villas is unparalleled.
​The serene atmosphere, surrounded by giant trees and the sounds of nature, offers the perfect escape from the hustle and bustle of city life. The service is exceptional, and the food—especially the traditional Kerala cuisine—is delicious and authentic. Whether you are looking for a peaceful retreat or a walk through the plantation trails, this place is a paradise for nature lovers and photographers. Highly recommended for anyone visiting Wayanad!"
reviews.author: shutterbugs wayanad

reviews.text: Philipkutty's Farm is an exceptional place. Anu and Aniamma are so warm and welcoming.  There is so much attention to detail in every aspect of the farm - the beautifully furnished villas, the gorgeous gardens and the artistically designed floating flower petal arrangements outside each villa.  The food is incredible - a feast for every meal  and all made with fresh coconuts, vegetables, herbs and spices grown on the farm. The setting is so beautiful and tranquil, with boats occasionally floating by and wonderful birds. We enjoyed the cookery demonstration and the visit to see the local village crafts.  A truly memorable experience and a real highlight of our trip to Kerala!
reviews.author: Lucinda Green

reviews.text: A one-of-a-kind stay on a small island farm, just a simple five-minute boat ride away. It’s a great place to unplug in a less busy area of the backwaters. The cottages are both beautiful and comfortable, and the food was amazing: unique Keralan dishes enjoyed together at a shared table with other guests and the family. Anu, her mom, and the staff were wonderfully warm, kind and attentive to all details even arranged a sunrise yoga session on the beautiful deck! I’d come back in a heartbeat.
reviews.author: Amel Ghouila

reviews.text: This slice of Kerala backwater heaven is faultless. Lazy rural vibes exuded in the form of the lake in front of the gorgeous heritage-style villas, lush coconut trees behind, and birds overhead. For every meal, an astonishing spread of traditional recipes Phillipkutty style, many ingredients coming from the farm. Warm, generous and thoughtful hosting by Anu, Aniamma and their fab team.
reviews.author: SL Wong

reviews.text: Such a special spot here. Great place for a few days to relax and enjoy coastal Kerala. The communal dining and food was a particular standout, absolutely delicious
reviews.author: Elspeth Hughes

reviews.text: Philipkutty's Farm was amazing! We received a warm and hospitable greeting from the family upon arrival. The meals that were prepared daily we're outstanding and showcase the local cuisine including fresh fish caught in the nearby lake. You will not regret staying at this quiet and beautiful homestead. We booked a cooking class, walked the grounds in the morning, took a sunset cruise during the evenings on the lake. The family has wonderful stories about the island on which the farm is located. Overall amazing experience and we definitely will visit again
reviews.author: Jonathan Silva

reviews.text:
An Unforgettable Escape to Yangsum Farm:
​We had an absolutely awesome time staying at Yangsum Farm in early March 2026. The property is nestled in a serene part of Sikkim, located in the beautiful village of Rinchenpong.
Our host and the owner of the farm was incredibly generous, kind-hearted, and amicable—truly a good soul. His genuine helpfulness and hospitality made us feel like we were on cloud nine. The property itself is stunning, boasting breathtaking mountain views, lush fruit orchards, and inviting treks and trails. One of the highlights was the food; the organic, farm-to-table meals were simply amazing.
​The cook, Meeraj, along with his colleague, prepared the most marvelous delicacies I have ever tasted. Not only was the food exceptional, but the staff was also soft-spoken, quick to serve, and extremely courteous.
​We fell in love with both the place and the people. Last but certainly not least, our guide for the trek to Rinchenpong Monastery—Meeraj's son, Darshan—was superb. Despite his youth, he possesses extraordinary knowledge about the entire area. He was friendly and helpful, always carrying a beautiful smile on his face.

​We are already missing this place dearly and will definitely make an effort to visit again!
reviews.author: Moon Tulip

reviews.text:
We had a great time staying at yangsum farm during christmas. the place is nestled in a quiter part of sikkim, in the beautiful village of rinchenpong.

Our host tashi & his family, are really helpful and hospitable. the property is awesome with mountain views, fruit orchards, treks and trails.  and the most amazing organic food - truly farm to table.

we fell in the love with the place and we were happy we had 4 nights here. i will definetly try to visit again.
reviews.author: Parin Shah

reviews.text: We stayed at this property for three days in November 2025 and was completely overwhelmed by the generous hospitality of the owner, Mr. Thendup, and his staff. Spread across many acres, they have their own farm with a variety of fruits and vegetables growing. The property offers a great, mighty view of Kanchendzonga from the room and garden. Rooms are extremely spacious with vintage look. All meals were freshly made using their farm‑grown vegetables. The owner is extremely polite and always there when the food is served, making you feel at home. Highly recommended if you are looking for a peaceful environment and want to spend leisure time. You can go for a jungle walk or do bird‑watching. This place is simply surreal.
reviews.author: Pragya Gupta

reviews.text:
By far the best stay in the west Sikkim
The property is beautiful which gives you organic food from their own farm fresh produce
The service and hospitality is unbeatable
The host is even better human if you are lucky to meet him while your stay
We stayed here for 2nights and had a memorable experience
Highly recommended to family’s and couples and to all who love nature in raw and rustic heritage farm
reviews.author: Rohit Bhatkar

reviews.text: Beautiful place, lovely people. Best stay. Had a great time. Thankyou very much. The owner and the staff are so lovely and helpful. Very warm people.
reviews.author: hiral bhatia

reviews.text: We stayed here for 3 days and it was an amazing experience. You need a full day just to relax and enjoy the property. Never miss any of the meals because the chef made excellent dishes which even my kids (who are very choosy) enjoyed wholeheartedly. Look, this is a vibrant resort which is well maintained but it is that rustic/nostalgic feeling (old style furniture, rooms with himachali architecture/design) which will leave a long lasting memory. Everything like the Norah Richards house, art gallery, pottery etc etc are nearby. So, at least spend 3 days and I will be back here soon!
reviews.author: Ashish S

reviews.text: Amazing place. We went during rains, lots of lychee on their property. Rooms were spacious and clean. The whole place was really aesthetic carefully selected artifacts. Friendly host🤗. Also amazinggggg cook. Knew how to prepare various delicious dishes. Staff also very friendly!
reviews.author: tarannum harisinganey

reviews.text:
Absolutely wonderful! If you are looking for something rustic, but amply comfortable, then the Mirage Andretta is just that. Nestled in greenery, with a beautiful view of snow  capped mountains, its just what one needs when you want a 'non-city' experience.
If not for the ambience, I would return for the amazing home cooked meals that were served. I was there for 6 nights and to deliver great tasting food consistently over that period, meal after meal, is no coincidence. They know how to please and have a great team from the local village, to deliver the right experience.
Thank you to all for making the stay memorable.
reviews.author: Suvir Khullar

reviews.text: Very nice property spread over a few acres, with cute cottages, nice rooms, artfully decorated has its own kitchen gwith art sourced from local artists. Perfect getawa for.  and quiet. Has a nice yoga room with windows all around. Has a rustic feel and yet all necessary amenities for a comfortable stay. The hosts are lovely people. Staff is great and helpful. Has its own kitchen garden and the food for the guests is delicious and healthy. Has plenty of outdoors seating areas surrounded by lovely landscaping, with several cute corners like a hammock canopy, a wooden swing etc. Some rooms have their own sit outs,  balconies or verandas. Would highly recommend for a quiet getaway.
reviews.author: Swati Vasudevan

reviews.text:
Mirage Andretta is such a beautiful boutique property done up with excellent taste. It’s more than anything you would expect. I spent 3 nights there this weekend and I haven’t stopped talking about it since.

My room had a rustic, european & cozy feel. It was too gorgeous to leave even for a few minutes to go down to enjoy the perfect scenery that surrounds the homestay 😍
The food was impeccable and the staff was warm and friendly and always there for you.

I would go to Andretta just to stay in Mirage in a heartbeat.

Special thanks to Denis and Manak for making my experience so smooth and enjoyable. Loved every bit of it :)
reviews.author: megha rawat

saratha-vilas.editorialSummary: Opulent mansion-turned-boutique hotel offering 8 upscale rooms, an outdoor pool & free breakfast.

reviews.text: An exceptional stay from start to finish! The warm hospitality and professionalism of the staff made us feel truly welcome. The hotel was impeccably clean, with beautiful heritage charm and a relaxing ambiance throughout. The swimming pool was excellent and one of the highlights of our stay. The food was outstanding, with every meal exceeding expectations. Overall, this was one of the best hotel experiences I have ever had, and created wonderful memories here. Highly recommended! Special thanks to the Manager Mr Kiran and Mr Nachi. Great….!
reviews.author: Sholendra Sholen

reviews.text:
Our stay at Saratha Vilas was one of those rare travel moments that feels truly special from beginning to end. The house itself is absolutely beautiful,elegant, full of character, and surrounded by a peaceful atmosphere that immediately makes you slow down and breathe differently. Every corner tells a story, and you really feel the soul of the place.

One of the highlights of our stay was the genuine sense of welcome we felt throughout our time there. Conversations flowed naturally, and there was a rare generosity in the way the place is shared with guests, simple, sincere, and unforgettable.

The food deserves a special mention, it was, without question, the best we had during our entire trip. Every meal was refined, generous, and full of flavor.

A truly special place that we would recommend without hesitation.
reviews.author: Céline Blomme

reviews.text: We stayed at Saratha Vilas for 3 nights. It’s a Chettiar Mansion redone very tastefully preserving the cultural aspects while providing modern conveniences that foreigners and NRIs expect. The place has been renovated and managed by two French architects who reside on the premises in winter and with whom we interacted extensively. We had lunch at 3 different Mansions and I have to say that we were very pleased with the boutique nature of Saratha, the warm welcome from the staff and our delicious breakfast and dinners! Highly recommend for the ultimate Chettinad experience.
reviews.author: raj rajgopal

reviews.text: We stayed there for one night and wished he had a longer stayed there longer. The room, common areas and surrounding were very well maintained. Food was all prepared within the premises kitchen and it was tasty. The staffs were very equipped to handle any requests. Alphie, the villa manager was well aware of the historical significance of the place, owners, villa and made our stay memorable. I’ll recommend it if you are look for peace, tradition and Chettinad set up.
reviews.author: Henry Victor

reviews.text: A short stay turned out to be a truly amazing experience staying in this villa. The cleanliness, facilities and most important the service was excellent. Everyone was courteous, friendly & efficient. I cant remember all their names but Nachi & Alphi was alway there to ensure we were comfortable and to advise on any questions we had. They were both friendly, polite and knowledgeable. Nachi  arranged a birthday cake for my husband and made his day by celebrating with all the staff and guests during breakfast. The chef Veerapandi was very passionate about his food. Both days he came out to meet us and explain the dishes which were very delicious. I would definitely like to come back and enjoy the hospitality this villa offers. Thank you everyone for the wonderful stay 🙏🏼🙏🏼🙏🏼
reviews.author: Nithi Prem

shahpura-haveli.editorialSummary: Genteel rooms, some with frescoes, in an elegant heritage hotel offering restaurants & bars.

reviews.text:
It's an awesome experience and feeling to have spend some time is such a heritage property!!!

Here is what the hotel chain has to say...
Escape the ordinary and embark on a journey that transcends time and tradition with Shahpura Hotels and Resorts. Their exquisite collection of hotels offers you to a world where heritage meets luxury, and every moment is a celebration of opulence and heritage.

Imagine waking up in a 300-year-old heritage haveli, steeped in history and adorned with timeless elegance. Picture yourself indulging in the lavish comforts of a modern boutique hotel, where contemporary design seamlessly blends with old-world charm. At Shahpura Hotels and Resorts, we offer a collage of experiences that redefine luxury and hospitality.

Where they can earn more star or improve.

# The road to the property is very very narrow which needs some thoughtful consideration.
# The meals (breakfast & lunch/dinner) included must have more options if buffet is not served.
# Room service/cleaning can be better.
#door handles are very inconvenient and everytime hurts the hand... no space for even fingers in between.
# shower should have a curtain when the toilet is attached.
reviews.author: Madhavi Shah

reviews.text:
We had a wonderful stay at Shahpura Haveli in Shahpura. The place has a beautiful village vibe, surrounded by hills and greenery that make it peaceful and relaxing. The terrace offers a stunning sunset view that we truly enjoyed.

Instead of a regular buffet, they offered freshly prepared food with good choices, which felt more personal and authentic. The staff were cooperative and very welcoming throughout our stay.

It’s also very kids-friendly, and our family had a great time exploring the heritage property. Overall, a lovely experience combining heritage charm, nature, and warm hospitality.
reviews.author: Deepak Mishra

reviews.text:
Palace of Soul, Beauty, and Exceptional Hospitality!

From the very first moment, our stay at Shaipur Haveli felt truly special. We were warmly welcomed by Dharmendra Singh, whose genuine hospitality immediately set the tone. He kindly took the time to give us a personal tour through the entire property, sharing the history and details of this beautifully restored palace with great passion.

The haveli itself is simply stunning. Every corner reflects thoughtful restoration and attention to detail. The rooms are individually designed, each with its own unique color palette and character, creating a sense of charm and authenticity that is rare to find.

The garden is a peaceful oasis—lovingly maintained, lush, and perfectly inviting. The pool area, surrounded by greenery, offers a serene retreat and is ideal for relaxing after a day of exploring.

What truly elevates the experience is the exceptional service. The team is attentive, warm, and effortlessly professional, making you feel cared for at all times without ever being intrusive.

This is not just a place to stay—it is an experience. I would return here without hesitation and very much look forward to coming back on my next trip to India.

Thank you so much!
reviews.author: Sandra Schüssler

reviews.text:
Had a wonderful experience at Shahpura Haveli! The property beautifully reflects traditional Rajasthani heritage with its elegant architecture and serene ambiance. The rooms were clean, spacious, and thoughtfully designed, offering both comfort and authenticity.

The restaurant was equally impressive — the food was absolutely delicious, with rich flavors and excellent presentation. The staff was courteous, attentive, and always ready to assist, which made the entire stay even more pleasant.

Overall, a perfect blend of hospitality, culture, and comfort. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a peaceful and memorable stay. Definitely a 5-star experience!

Hats off to Mr Dharmender Singh and Mr Bharat at restaurant. Thanks
reviews.author: Shrey Nagpal

reviews.text:
Fabulous lunch in this rural setting, music dancing and four courses of Indian specialities.  Dharmendra looked after our every need and was professional and efficient.
Great experience to break our journey from Delhi to Jaipur
reviews.author: claire cochran

reviews.text: We stayed here from 18.05.26 to 20.05.26. It was an wonderful experience. Hosts are fantastic, foods were very good. The place is so beautiful!
reviews.author: Manas Patra

reviews.text:
I went here after being referred by a friend.
Situated in a nice cosy area with beautiful views overlooking the hills and there is a very famous attraction in a walkable distance named chimney.
The food was beautiful and the uncle and aunty who hosts the home stay is beautiful by heart ♥️🙌such a lovely experience it was.
I been to many places and have stayed in so many places but for some reason my heart is already pushing me to back to this place
Just because the hospitality was magical ..
thanks and see you soon 🙌
And forgot to mention it’s very clean and very well maintained.
reviews.author: nikhil alias

reviews.text: I must say the room the view the service everything are awesome, it literally feel like a home . Aunty and uncle they are so nice people so humble also and aunty ka haath ka khana so testy ❤❤❤❤ I recommend everyone too must visit🥰🥰🥰🥰
reviews.author: Roma Besra

reviews.text: It's a very great homestay...it offers a great view,and people here are really humble polite and very gentle...they really care about our comfort...and the view from here is mind blowing.❤️
reviews.author: Krish Zaman
