Below the fort lie the beautiful Narmada ghats, where pilgrims perform rituals and evening aarti creates a calm, divine atmosphere.
Maheshwar is also world-famous for Maheshwari handloom sarees, known for their elegance and durability. Visiting local weavers near the fort is a unique cultural experience.
The best time to visit Maheshwar Fort is from October to March, especially during sunrise or sunset, when the river and fort glow with golden hues. Overall, Maheshwar Fort is a must-visit destination for history lovers, spiritual seekers, photographers, and anyone looking for peace and heritage in one place. 🌅🙏
reviews.author: Indori wanderer

reviews.text:
What u will get
1. Amazing heritage site
2. Lord Shiva's blessings
3. Photogenic place for sunrise n sunset
4. Shop for Maheshwari silk sarees

What you will loose
1. Weight - as if need to walk from parking
2. Stress - it's a calm n quite place
3. Time & Money - if visiting such places is waste of time & Saree shopping is tedious task for you.....

Don't worry, it's a good place to visit, while coming from Ujjain, I will recommend this detour....
reviews.author: bhushan suryawanshi

reviews.text:
Ahilyabai Devi Mahshwar Fort, its a perfect example & amazing Ghats on the River Narmada built by Ahilyabai Holkar & dynasty.

This is part of the fort & you can experience the huge monuments, architectural windows doors & carved stone work.

Must visit place & best time to explore is early morning before 10am, you will experience the peaceful, calm morning here.
reviews.author: ganesh gajre

jims-jungle-retreat.generativeSummary: Spacious, clean, jungle-themed cottages boasting pools and safaris, along with spa treatments.
jims-jungle-retreat.editorialSummary: Refined cottages on forested grounds offering wildlife sightings, open-air dining & an outdoor pool.

reviews.text:
I visited Jim’s Jungle Retreat because I wanted comfortable accommodation, high-quality local food, and a strong chance of seeing as much wildlife as possible in Jim Corbett National Park.

Jim’s Jungle Retreat exceeded my expectations on all three. The accommodation was cosy and well-appointed, the food was excellent, and the whole experience felt genuinely connected to nature, both at the retreat itself and on the game drives with the naturalist team.

I had also recently bought a new lens for my camera and wanted the opportunity to test it on truly diverse and impressive wildlife. I was absolutely able to do that here. The guides helped me see everything from tigers and elephants to monkey families and an incredible variety of birds.

I would absolutely recommend it to anyone wanting to see wildlife while also enjoying a relaxing stay and memorable food.
reviews.author: Danyn Kalidas

reviews.text:
Staying at this resort felt like stepping into a real jungle paradise. Surrounded by lush greenery, the entire place offers a truly immersive natural experience….perfect for anyone wanting to escape the chaos of daily life. The cottages are beautifully designed, elegant yet cozy, and blend seamlessly with the surroundings, giving a luxurious yet close-to-nature feel.

The ambience is incredibly peaceful and calming, with the soothing sounds of nature enhancing every moment. It’s the kind of place where you can truly relax, unwind, and recharge.

What makes the experience even better is the variety of amenities available. From a refreshing swimming pool to a quiet library, a relaxing spa, and fun indoor activities like board games, chess, and even hand hockey….there’s something for everyone to enjoy.

If you're looking for a serene getaway that combines nature, comfort, and thoughtful amenities, this is definitely a must-stay destination for peace and tranquility.
reviews.author: Aizan Hadi

reviews.text:
Some places leave an impression that lingers long after you have checked out. Jim's Jungle Retreat at Corbett National Park is one of them, and I cannot recommend it highly enough.

From the very first point of contact, the standard of hospitality here is exceptional. Manoj guided us through our safari and stay options during the booking process with such care and expertise that we felt in safe hands before we had even arrived. That warmth carried through our entire stay, with Dev ensuring we wanted for nothing throughout. A particular mention must go to Chef Deepak, who is nothing short of a star. My partner was recovering from food poisoning contracted at a previous hotel, and Chef Deepak went above and beyond to ensure he was properly nourished and looked after. That level of personal care is rare and will not be forgotten.

Our naturalist, Lauvkush, was outstanding. His depth of knowledge about the surrounding wildlife and ecosystem was remarkable, and we came away having learnt so much. We had not dared hope to see tigers, yet thanks to his expertise and guidance, we were lucky enough to witness exactly that. He was instrumental in making that once-in-a-lifetime moment possible.

The retreat itself is beautifully designed with clear intention in every detail: thoughtfully laid out, immaculately maintained, and deeply in harmony with its natural surroundings. In the gorgeous Carpet Sahib Cafe, I came across a book celebrating the most extraordinary hotels in India. In my honest opinion, Jim's Jungle Retreat belongs in its pages without question.

Leaving was genuinely painful. To any future guests, I would say this: do not rush. Take the time to soak in the property, engage with the wonderful staff, and ask about their sustainability initiatives, it is well worth understanding the ethos behind this remarkable place.

Jim's Jungle Retreat is a true haven, and we hope very much to return.
reviews.author: Jas Kaur

reviews.text:
This was probably one of the best places I’ve stayed in while doing a safari holiday. The whole experience was almost surreal. We wanted to stay at the Taj but couldn’t get rooms because of a wedding (what serious safari resort allows this?!)… and I thank God that we found this place instead. The photos on their website do not do the place justice. The family cottage was extremely convenient and spacious. The staff (every single one of them) were very courteous and professional. They took hospitality to a whole other standard. Regarding the food - it was near perfection. The variety, the service, the taste - unbeatable. We were apprehensive about the cuisine as it’s new to us but every meal was excellent. The safaris were very good - the guides are knowledgeable and made great efforts plus the tea breaks in the forest were lovely. The best part about Jim’s is the absolute peace and quiet - it gives you a good forest experience. If you’re the sort of tourist who enjoys loud noise and music and late night ruckus, please do us a favor and stay in the town so that those of us who love the sound of nothing but nature can truly enjoy JJR.
WiFi connectivity is very good. Poolside wildlife films with drinks and snacks were excellent.
Special mention about their brilliant logo and the tagline “seek the tiger, find the forest”. I love that they are about caring for the ecology and the forest. I was even fine to not go to dhikala zone thanks to the Jim’s jungle experience. Would love to go back… I hope the neighbouring areas don’t become overcrowded and spoil the area (it’s already starting to). I hope the country can preserve and respect the forest and enable more places like Jim’s.
reviews.author: Anju Sabu

reviews.text: An excellent place to stay and feel the jungle. Every effort is made to make you feel pampered. Delicious food. Warm hospitality and comfy rooms.
reviews.author: Khan M K

reviews.text:
The property is genuinely beautiful and very well maintained with great attention to the tiniest of details. The staff stood out for being responsive, warm, and always willing to help. All the rooms were spotless, thoughtfully laid out, and done up with great taste.

The food was THE highlight of the trip. Every meal felt well-crafted and satisfying.

If there’s one small thing that could elevate the experience further, it would be adding a good coffee machine at the cafe/kitchen and, if possible, have an organized covered car park.
reviews.author: Vishal Menon

reviews.text:
The whole hotel is quite beatiful, service is very good. Staff is always there to fulfil you every wish. Our room was quite dark, we were medium happy with it.

I feel the prices are quite high. Especially the boat trip is way too expensive for what is offered.

The massage therapist was really exceptionally good. I would book it again every time.
reviews.author: Ribon Baercraft

reviews.text:
The place has a beautiful aesthetic with grand, old rajbari vibes that create a unique and charming atmosphere. I have visited once for lunch and stayed overnight once as well, and overall it was a pleasant experience. The ambience is definitely one of the highlights. However, the room prices now feel quite high, and it would be great if they could be a bit more considerate in that aspect. The lunch was tasty and well-prepared, but since there were many guests that day, the service felt a bit rushed. Unfortunately, by the time the after-meal paan was being served, it had run out. When guests are paying a good amount, such finishing touches should be available for everyone, regardless of the crowd.

Overall, it was a good experience mainly because of the lovely ambience, but a few improvements in pricing consideration, service management, and proper stock planning would make it even better.
reviews.author: Kaveri Ghosh

reviews.text:
The Rajbari at Bawali is located an hour from the Taj Bengal in the general direction of Diamond Harbor. This 300 year old heritage property is built on 3 acres of land including 2 natural waterbodies. The Rajbari Bawali belonged to the Bawali Mondal family that had land grants going back to the time of Akbar.

The Rajbari Bawali, popular among travelers, is celebrated for its spacious rooms adorned with vintage decor and modern amenities, exuding comfort and historic charm. Service earns rave reviews for warmth and attentiveness, though some note minor maintenance issues. The hotel's immaculate cleanliness and regal atmosphere with lush courtyards are often highlighted.

While the unique heritage charm justifies premium rates for many, some caution about expense. Amenities like pools and a spa are praised, despite suggestions for minor improvements&
The service you wish for in a 5* hotel, but never get
The kind of service you would wish for in a five star hotel, but almost never get! This “heritage hotel” is in a beautifully restored palace. The rooms are comfortable (pay for the upgradevif you want light and silence) and the food is good, with Bengali (highly recommended), standard Indian and Western fare. They have beer, wine, cocktails and spirits.
reviews.author: Dr Soham Seth

reviews.text:
Had a lovely weekend stay.would recommended everyone to spend a day or two. Very refreshing.
It's an experience to enjoy the traditional culture and food
And to feel the experience of Rajbari's to it's fullest.
This was our 2nd visit to Bawali. All staffs and people working are very well behaved and very curteceous
reviews.author: Mitul Das

reviews.text:
We had a fabulous stay at the Premkunj palace. We got there in the evening and was warmly greeted by our welcoming host, Mr Bhupendra Sing. The swimming pool and the courtyard was all lit up. It felt really cozy and special.
We had a wonderful sleep in the beautiful and very spacious bedroom. Waking up to the sound of birds singing and seeing the trees blossoming in the courtyard was such a loving moment. Breakfast was excellent and the best way to start the day.                       We really enjoyed the peace of the area. Such a contrast to the hustle and the bustle of downtown Udaipur. When we later moved because Premkunj was fully booked we regretted having to leave. Udaipur was so noisy we did not sleep well there. We highly recommend anyone booking Premkunj. It is much better to stay 15 minutes outside of town and having the peace and quiet and the beautiful nature surrounding you rather than being sleepless in Udaipur.
We really hope to be back at Premkunj soon again!
reviews.author: Anna Bohlmark

reviews.text: It's a hidden gem located at the end of the road. Quiet, absolutely gorgeous with a great view of the mountain & Sajjangarh wildlife sanctuary. The interiors are beautiful, size of the rooms allows you to lounge around comfortable sitting areas. The place also has a well-maintained pool. Local food served at the place is flavourful and delicious. The place is walking distance from Badi Lake and Bahubali Hills. Would highly recommend to anyone looking a quiet getaway (long or short) at a place that allows you to unwind and recharge.
reviews.author: Hans Vivek

reviews.text: Really it was great experience to be at Prem kunj Udaipur. Hospitaliry,behaviour of staff,ambience and quality of food is exceelent. Cheff Pooran ji served dishes  as per our choice. Sumputous food.Top of it owner sh. Bhupendra singh ji chundawat is very cordial and respect giving. Rajdeepak Rastogi
reviews.author: Rajdeepak Rastogi

reviews.text: Loved this place. An absolute hidden gem. From location to service to the decor, it's just perfect. Would recommend  it to anyone looking for a quiet but super enjoyable weekend getaway or a long term one!
reviews.author: Bindu Sharma

reviews.text: Absolute perfection. Every detail is amazing and the comfort of the guests is thought out and cared for. Mr Singh and his team went above and beyond to make sure we were happy in this beautiful, quiet and lovely home.
reviews.author: Nancy Kinyanjui

rakkh-palampur.editorialSummary: Unfussy quarters in a relaxed hillside hotel offering mountain views, a spa & a rooftop terrace.

reviews.text:
A truly experiential activity oriented luxury resort. True to their name Rakkh — Saahi and his entire team (Raman, Rakesh, Subedo aunty,  Akhil, Nirmal, the kitchen/restaurant staff and not to forget Talli) kept the promise of providing the most authentic Himachali experience and being so passionate about what they do!

What stood out the most for us was how deeply rooted they are in their culture, how they enlighten others about it with pride and how they are building a community. From the pahadi treks, handloom weaving sessions, DIY counters to the Himachali rasoi (never had anything like this before)- all curated to perfection.

The room was absolutely stunning and super spacious, had the most gorgeous bathtub. The little details-from the sustainable toiletries, Hibiscus tea, copper bottles to their coffee book- reflects on how they have gone the extra mile. The warmth and care shown by everyone was impressive . Saahi was so well versed with the history, area and property - an absolute delight meeting him and exceptional management on his part which ensures smooth functioning despite the steep paths😀

The sky is your limit with the different activities all included in your package, so much abundance. More power to the property, can’t wait to be back! Miss everyone already🫶🏻
reviews.author: Surbhi

reviews.text:
Stayed at Rakkh Resort, Palampur on 23rd Jan 2026, and it turned into an unforgettable experience. That night, I witnessed my first-ever snowfall, and it felt nothing short of magical.

The property is beautifully located, peaceful, and perfectly blends luxury with nature. The staff was warm and welcoming, making the stay even more comfortable. Experiencing snowfall in such a serene setting made the moment truly special.
reviews.author: Manpreet

reviews.text:
A truly refreshing experience.

Rakkh Resort is a beautiful blend of nature, luxury, and mindful living. The ambience with the Dhauladhar mountain views is breathtaking and instantly helps you slow down and reconnect with yourself.

The staff hospitality is exceptional warm, attentive, and genuinely caring. The food is fresh, flavorful, and thoughtfully prepared.

What really stands out are the activities that combine adventure with nature, making the stay both energizing and peaceful.

A perfect place to pause, reset, and experience the beauty of the mountains.

— Life Coach Ramandiep
reviews.author: Ramandiep Singh Tanejaa

reviews.text: We celebrated our 7th wedding anniversary at Rakkh Resort and had a truly memorable, peaceful break as a couple. The setting is stunning, the cottages are cozy and private, and the staff—especially Sunny, Rohit, Anshul, Anil, Safedo aunty and Nirmal—went out of their way to make our stay feel special and personalised. From the delicious food to the quiet evenings in the hills, it was exactly the relaxed romantic getaway we were hoping for, and we left already planning our next visit.
reviews.author: Reet k

reviews.text:
It was a very lovely and different experience to stay at Rakkh Resort.
No TV and wi-fi is very good concept to enjoy the nature and spend quality time with family members.
Very much child friendly resort to engage them in doing different nature related and adventure activities.
Food was excellent with differently crafted menu.
DIY activities like maggi, soda, juice, pizza and barbeque making was cherry on top.
Entire staff was very helpful and corteous.

Thank you for such a memorable experience
reviews.author: jigar patel

waterwoods-kabini.editorialSummary: Refined resort offering river views & wildlife safaris, plus a spa, pools & dining.

reviews.text: Excellent stay overall. The housekeeping team, especially Guru and his colleagues, were outstanding and ensured everything was impeccably maintained. The food was also excellent, with a great variety and quality throughout our stay. The only area for improvement would be the property itself, which feels a bit dated and could benefit from some renovation considering the price point. Nevertheless, we had a wonderful experience.
reviews.author: Vidya R

reviews.text:
Overall, the place offered a pleasant experience — the ambience, food, and rooms were all quite good. However, there are a few areas that need significant improvement.

The hospitality around the safari service was disappointing. The staff were rude, particularly when we chose not to book a safari through them. A guest's decision should always be met with professionalism and respect, regardless of whether they opt for a service or not.

The safari itself was underwhelming — the only major wildlife sighting was an elephant, which did not justify the cost. We felt it was not good value for money.

On the in-room facilities front, the internet was non-functional throughout our stay. The TV also did not work initially, and only after remote was hit twice we were able to use it. These are basic amenities that guests expect to work without having to follow up.

We hope the management takes this feedback constructively and works toward improving the guest experience across all touchpoints.
reviews.author: Shruthi Prasad

reviews.text:
★☆☆☆☆

One of the most disappointing resort experiences I have had.

This property markets itself as a 4-star resort, but based on the room condition, amenities, service standards, and overall upkeep, it feels closer to a 1-star property. The only thing going for it is the beautiful river view. Beyond that, the experience was extremely underwhelming.

The room itself was poorly maintained. We found lizards inside the room, the linens and curtains appeared dirty, and the washroom had a persistent unpleasant smell. Even basic maintenance was lacking—the fan switch in our room was not functioning properly. We had booked a twin-bed room, but the mattresses were of poor quality and there was a large gap between the two beds, making it uncomfortable to sleep.

The food was another major disappointment. Although lunch and dinner are advertised as being included, don't expect the kind of buffet spread normally associated with a 4-star resort. Guests are served a preset menu with virtually no choice. For the price charged, this felt extremely restrictive and poor value for money.

The hospitality and management standards were equally disappointing. During our stay, we observed the property supervisor openly consuming alcohol in the restaurant area. Whether or not this violates any policy, it certainly did not reflect the level of professionalism one would expect from a property charging premium rates.

The overall property appears far less impressive than what is portrayed in its promotional photographs. The panoramic images of the rooms, pool, and common areas create expectations that the actual experience does not meet. The resort feels dated, poorly maintained, and significantly overpriced for what it offers.

What makes the experience even more frustrating is the price. We paid approximately ₹22,000 for a single night's stay and genuinely felt cheated. The experience was nowhere near what one would expect at that price point. We have stayed at budget hotels that offered cleaner rooms, better maintenance, and more professional service.

I am honestly surprised by the number of 5-star reviews for this property. Our experience was so poor that it is difficult to reconcile it with the ratings we saw online. Based on our stay, I cannot imagine a family having the experience we had and rating this property highly.

Considering the premium pricing, the value for money is extremely poor. I would not recommend Waterwoods Kabini to friends or family. If you're planning a stay in Kabini, consider Serai Kabini next door instead. At a similar price point, it offers a far better experience, facilities, service standards, and overall value.
reviews.author: Hemant Mangal

reviews.text:
One of the best resorts we’ve ever stayed at 😊 . We were a family of four (parents with two small children) and stayed for 2 nights. Special thanks to their staff to make this trip so memorable 🙏🙂.

The food 🍽️, staff 🤝, services 🌟, and the place itself, everything is just excellent. They also have a huge open farm with fruit trees 🌳🍊, and the river flowing next to it 🌊 adds to the magic.

The peace and serenity of this place truly blew us away 😌✨. We had an absolutely wonderful experience and made beautiful memories. A must-visit place! Highly recommended ❤️😊.
reviews.author: anshu handa

reviews.text:
The biggest problem with this property is the upkeep and maintenance of rooms. It looked dated and old. The AC was not working properly. Bathroom fixtures were old and not working at 100%. My room costed 32K INR per night (family room, magnolia). At this price, I expected 5 star quality but it was 2.5 star.

Other than this, I have no complains. Service by all staff was excellent. The in-house kitchen is very good. It's a set menu but well thought out for everyone's needs. And the best part - heavenly location. Right on the bank of the amazingly beautiful Kabini river.
reviews.author: Nishant Kumar

reviews.text:
We stayed at Amar Mahal on 25th February 2026 to celebrate my husband’s birthday. Unfortunately, what was meant to be a special occasion turned into a very disappointing experience.

The only good part of this property is the greenery and scenic surroundings outside the rooms. The campus looks nice. But hospitality is where this hotel fails badly.

Our interactions with the day-shift front desk staff member (I believe his name was Ritesh) were consistently rude and dismissive from the very first phone call. Every time we approached the front desk for assistance, the response felt curt and unhelpful. There was no warmth, no proactive support, and no sense of guest care.

The most serious incident happened when I slipped in the bathroom due to an extremely slippery floor surface and injured my leg. There was visible bleeding. We immediately called the front desk seeking help. We were told that since we had come in our own car, my husband could go out and arrange bandages and ointment himself.

There was no offer of first aid, no immediate assistance, and no apology. When I personally showed the injury and raised the concern, the response was simply that the tiles would be “checked.” The lack of empathy in that moment was honestly shocking.

I have previously stayed at Orchha Palace & Convention Centre, and the service there was far more professional and guest-oriented. Given the size and positioning of Amar Mahal, I expected much better.

A hotel is defined by how it treats guests, especially in situations involving safety. Based on our experience, that standard was not met.

I would not recommend this property to anyone.
reviews.author: Himanshi Chopra

reviews.text:
An Unforgettable Royal Stay at Amar Mahal Orcha from 17 to 21 December 2025

This year marked a very special milestone for us, the Golden Jubilee of our MBBS admission 1975 batch of MLB Medical College Jhansi, completing 50 glorious years.

To celebrate this once in a lifetime occasion, we began planning almost a year in advance. After exploring several hotels in Jhansi as well as Orchha, we finally chose Amar Mahal, Orchha and we can say with absolute pride that we have no regrets whatsoever. We stayed there from 17 to 21 December 2025 and returned yesterday only.

The vast beautiful lawns simply mesmerized our batchmates. Hotel Amar unfolded before us as an expansive campus...grand, elegant and truly impressive leaving a lasting sense of admiration.

The rooms were large, impeccably clean,
tastefully furnished and equipped with lavish attached washrooms offering both luxury and comfort.

The restaurant and room services were flawless, not once did we feel any shortcoming, whether during breakfast, lunch or dinner.

Every member of the staff was courteous, attentive, and genuinely eager to help, making us feel more like honoured guests than visitors.

The gentle flow of the Betwa nearby and the spiritual calm of Ram Raja Temple added a quiet grace to our celebration.

General Manager Sh Sandeep Bamonia

A special and heartfelt appreciation goes to Mr. Sandeep, General Manager, whose personal involvement, ever available presence and cooperative attitude ensured an entirely error free, stress free experience. His attention to even the smallest details made a lasting impression on all of us.

Highly recommended property for group stays, celebrations, and anyone seeking comfort blended with royal elegance.

Full marks to Amar Mahal and its outstanding team.
Dr Nita Verman
Dr S K Verman
Verman Hospital
Mathura
Dr R N Tandon
Dr Vibha Tandon.
New Delhi
reviews.author: Dr. S.k. Verman

reviews.text: Remotely located at about 18 Km from Jhansi railway station. About 10-12 Km one has to pass through jungle. Good for film shooting with good number of hotels. Orchha is on the bank of river Betwa, good place but semi clean status. So is the Raja Ram temple, narrow lanes of kasba type market, availability of medical shop poor, less dense population. Amar mahal hotel is very nice, beautiful gardens and cleanliness.
reviews.author: N.R.K. Sinha

reviews.text: Visited this place in early May. We were here to celebrate my parents anniversary. Property is a set up on a  very luxurious space. The the palace is set on a single story building and they have courtyard behind every wing. So they are three wings in total and you get a very total view from each of the wings. If you see the aesthetic wise, a very beautiful place, click pictures here and overall, it gives you very heritage and antique vibe which I totally loved about this place. We also placed an order from the restaurant for the cake which they prepared for us on a very timely based, and then they also help us arrange to put a set up and everything so overall my experience was very good. I would recommend this place for someone who wants Royal vibe on a great price at the same time. It’s a peaceful and calm stay.
reviews.author: somya dwivedi

reviews.text: Amar Mahal was the dream come true when it came to venue for my sister’s wedding. The natural views are absolutely gorgeous and even more when decorated. The hotel manager Sandeep ji was extremely patient and understanding throughout the event. We couldn’t imagine a better place or a better team to work with during such an intimate event. Would recommend anyone looking for a perfect venue to check out Amar Mahal!!!
reviews.author: Diksha Banka

neeleshwar-hermitage.editorialSummary: Thatch-roof cottages in a tranquil beach resort with dining, a spa & a pool, plus yoga & meditation.

reviews.text: Second visit to this unique spot. Exquisite location on a vast, deserted, beautiful beach. Luxury boutique hotel atmosphere. Excellent food, faultless service from a staff team for whom nothing is too much trouble. Dining under the stars on the beach with your feet in the sand. Is there a better place to escape the Northern European winter?
reviews.author: Ralph Harrisson

reviews.text: The property is located in a quite fishing hamlet next to a public beach. The cottages are well furnished and maintained. We used the garden facing cottage which was very comfortable for a family of four. Though the bathrooms are semi open the experience is unique. The property is more than 18 years old but very well maintained, clean and have a wonderful staff. The ambience with the coconut trees and plants gives a very peaceful and tranquil feeling, away from the hustle and bustle of city life. The chef and his team in the restaurant were excellent and extremely accommodative to our dietary requirements (Vegan). The local cuisine prepared by them was taste and awesome. The staff were extremely courteous and helpful.   Had a wonderful Kerala ayurvedic massage along with free consultation with an ayurvedic doctor who gave us some very informative suggestions for better health. Overall, had a wonderful holiday at Neeleshwar Hermitage and we are looking forward for our next visit.
reviews.author: Capt. Prashant Patil

reviews.text: We loved our stay at The Hermitage.  Small and luxurious, it is a gem on the Arabian Sea. The staff are wonderful, the food is fabulous, the setting is amazing.  I enjoyed the yoga every morning, and swam in the infinity pool.  We cycled the small road to the Harbour, and walked the unspoilt beach.  We saw a a large nest with a sea eagle guarding her two chicks.  It is such a charming, lovely place.
reviews.author: Wendy Robinson

reviews.text:
We stayed for 5 nights and had an excellent time! This is a small peaceful resort away from the crowds of the more well known areas in southern Kerala. The property is beautiful- well maintained grounds, spacious and comfortable rooms and beds, common areas with gorgeous classical architecture and a very nice pool. The beach is clean, easily accessible and as far as the eye can see only palm trees are visible. We were often the only ones in the ocean.
Everyone on the staff including management is courteous and friendly from housekeeping to servers at the 2 restaurants. We ate regularly at the beachside restaurant and especially appreciate the service from Avinash and another tall young man both of whom were warm and always ready to accommodate our preferences. The food was excellent, especially the fresh seafood.
We had several Ayurvedic massages and they were also excellent. We also went on a couple of excursions. We enjoyed the guided trip to a small agricultural village which offered a small glimpse of daily life including a visit to the local ritual grounds, viewing the harvesting of beetlenuts and a visit to a school.
reviews.author: Michael Appel

reviews.text:
We enjoyed a truly remarkable three-night stay in a Sea Villa at Neeleshwar Hermitage. From the stunning infrastructure to the pristine beach and the genuine cordiality of the staff, every element was exceptional.
A special note of thanks goes to Captain Ajeesh and his team; their attentive service and hospitality were outstanding and deserve high praise.
Our only suggestion for improvement concerns the spa. We noticed that sessions were consistently 10-15 minutes shorter than the durations listed in the brochure. It would be helpful to either update the brochure or ensure the full time is honoured. Aside from this minor point, it was a wonderful experience, and we look forward to returning.
reviews.author: Sid Bala J

reviews.text:
Stayed there 2 days in May 2026 (1st and 2nd) with family in heritage room. Room is really large and nicely decorated. Near and clean, spacious bathroom. Surroundings environment is really nice.
Service is ok but needs improvement.
Once you call for help they may say coming but no one turns up. This is my experience. You may need to visit reception.
We did not expect much service either. Really like the surrounding. Beautiful flowers. Must say about its gardens. It is wonderful!
reviews.author: Ranajit Ghosh

reviews.text:
ok ok experience. no wifi available, service also not good as no one came to help with our luggage. water kettle was also not working in the rooms. otherwise the property was good with pretty flowers and good location.
There were welcome drinks and cookie platter promised while booking from cleartrip but the hotel staff behaved unknowingly as if they are listening for the first time.

Food is above average but quantity is less.
reviews.author: Rishant Verma

reviews.text: Our stay at Kluney Manor, Ooty was absolutely delightful. Nestled amidst lush greenery and misty hills, the resort offers a peaceful and refreshing escape from city life. The rooms were spacious, clean, and well-maintained, with stunning views that made mornings truly special. The staff were warm, courteous, and always ready to help, making us feel genuinely welcomed. The food was delicious and comforting, perfectly complementing the calm hill-station vibe. Overall, Kluney Manor is an excellent choice for anyone looking for a relaxing, nature-filled stay in Ooty.
reviews.author: Sri Ratheesh

reviews.text:
Large area and each set of rooms in different places (not nearby).
Amazing view... Very good gardening..
Best place to stay..
Difficult to go out of you don't have car.

Rooms little old but they are maintaining properly..
reviews.author: Chellamuniraj Vallimuthu

reviews.text:
⭐ 1/5 — Absolutely Terrible Experience. Do NOT Stay Here.

I rarely write negative reviews, but this hotel left me with no choice. This was genuinely one of the worst hotel experiences I have ever had, and I feel it is my responsibility to warn other travellers.

🚫 Pathetic Service: The staff was rude, unhelpful, and completely indifferent to guest needs. There was zero hospitality — you are made to feel like a burden, not a paying guest.

🍽️ Food Quality — Inedible: The food served was shockingly bad. No freshness, no taste, no standard. It was clear that quality is simply not a concern for this establishment.

🚿 No Hot Water: A basic necessity — hot water — was not available. Taking a cold shower is not a choice when you are paying for a hotel room. This is absolutely unacceptable.

📵 No WiFi: In 2026, a hotel without reliable WiFi is a red flag. The connection was non-existent. Whether for work or leisure, this makes the stay completely unusable.

This hotel fails on every basic standard — service, food, hygiene, and connectivity. Save your money and your sanity. There are far better options available. I would give 0 stars if I could.
reviews.author: vivek lakshmanan

astroport-sariska.displayName: Astroport Sariska Resort & Spa by Eight Continents
astroport-sariska.editorialSummary: Cottages in a serene resort featuring mountain treks, astronomy tours & cultural programs.

reviews.text: Mr.Sonu had been super helpful during our visit giving us good service keeping our kids in mind. Mr. Shekhawat had been amazing as well.. ensuring that our participation in all the services like nature walk, pottery, astronomy sessions. It’s great to enjoy such an amazing hospitality with Astroport!! Thank you everyone out there, who made our trip worth it!!
reviews.author: Ramganesh Iyer

reviews.text:
Astroport has an amazing team ensuring your stay is comfortable and memorable.
Food is excellent, steeped in local culture.
Rahul is dedicated to make sure the Astronomy session is a success and always enthusiastic to explore a little more. While Shekhawat ji is a veteran at the property sharing his precious knowledge of local flaura and fauna.
The whole team led by their GM  is very pleasant and always available. The property itself is very well maintained and Rooms are spacious with a very earthy aesthetic. Not to be missed!
reviews.author: Preeti Naithani

reviews.text: Astroport was an amazing experience! We only stayed for 2 nights and would have liked to stay longer. The resort is convenient for the Sariska Tiger reserve and the stargazing experience led by Navjot was very good. All the staff were very helpful and friendly especially Shubham who also assisted to explain menus and when there were some language issues. The overall experience as a first time visitor to India was amazing.
reviews.author: Gabi Gabi

reviews.text: Had a wonderful stay at Astroport Sariska Resort. The peaceful surroundings, clean rooms, delicious food, and friendly staff made the experience memorable. The stargazing activity was unique and exciting. Perfect place to relax, connect with nature, and enjoy quality time with family and friends. Highly recommended!
reviews.author: Jaydeep Panchal

reviews.text:
The food was great. Hansraj and Rakesh both served us well, great host and good food.
Navjyot from the stargazing bit was thorough and we had a wonderful experience watching the constellants and the planets along with the the session on how it all evolved and moves.
Bhagwan Singh ji gave us an enthusiastic nature trip along with beautiful sunset.
Overall had a wonderful experience.
Thanks Astroport.
reviews.author: Aayushi Agrawal

barefoot-havelock.editorialSummary: Tech-free quarters in a laid-back beachside resort surrounded by rainforest, plus dining & a spa.

reviews.text:
Long Review Alert!!

This place is simply awesome for most parts but comes with some caveats.

Best Part - the private beach access to Radhanagar Beach. It’s 500m away from the public entrance and it’s way less crowded at this properties beach.

Overall hotel property is awesome with lots of greenery, bird sightings and lots of trees. Their reception area, which also has a cozy bar, is great place to hang out.

We stayed in their largest cottage, sadly the room is decent sized but washroom and the deck was huge. Those were hardly usable because you’ll spend most of the time at the beach.

We did their private dinner by the beach for special occasion. It’s a decent experience, nice decor with average food. They didn’t serve lobster in this dinner even after giving preferences was surprising. This dinner was 18k and didn’t provide value for money.

Hospitality is mixed as well, reception staff and the bartenders are warm and welcoming but other staff definitely need some training.
reviews.author: Swapnil Mahajan

reviews.text:
Barefoot at Havelock is blessed with one of the most spectacular locations in India. Situated right next to Radhanagar Beach—often called the best beach in Asia—the setting is truly magical. Surrounded by dense forest, the property feels like a secluded nature escape and is perfect for those looking to disconnect and immerse themselves in the outdoors.

However, while the location is unbeatable, the accommodation falls short of expectations. The rooms are extremely dated and lack even basic hotel amenities such as dental kits, slippers, vanity kits, proper hooks in the washrooms, etc. Both the rooms and bathrooms are very poorly lit, creating an eerie rather than romantic ambience. The furniture and overall interiors clearly need refurbishment.

The dining experience was another disappointment. Breakfast is à la carte with very limited options and almost no variation across days, which becomes monotonous during a longer stay. The restaurant service also needs improvement—while the reception staff is warm and welcoming, the servers appeared ill-informed about the menu and struggled to answer basic questions. Given the large number of staff on site, better training and efficiency at the restaurant would significantly enhance the guest experience.

That said, the location remains Barefoot’s biggest and undeniable USP. We would love to visit again for the beach and natural surroundings alone, but would think twice before making it our final choice unless improvements are made to the rooms and food service. Unfortunately, the exclusivity of the location leaves guests with few alternatives, making one accept sub-par accommodation and dining in exchange for an otherwise extraordinary setting.
reviews.author: Ambika Maitra

reviews.text: Absolutely loved my stay at Barefoot Resort! This resort is a perfect, tranquil blend of forest and beach. The location on Radhanagar Beach means you can witness one of the most beautiful sunsets right from the property. The atmosphere is incredibly quiet and relaxing, and the entire staff is amazing. I must give a special shout-out to the chefs—the food they prepare is simply outstanding! A truly wonderful experience.
reviews.author: Trupti Shetty

reviews.text:
Radhanagar Beach is in the top 3 beaches I've been to in the world - and I've been to a few! The location for Barefoot is therefore stunning. The separation from the restaurant/accommodation and the beach is incredible - beautiful big trees!

Our welcome was fab and throughout the stay, all staff were so lovely.

Room was great - Andaman villa lived up to expectations.
Restaurant - food was incredible and the team always ensured you were looked after.
Spa - I had a mud wrap and it was a great experience.
Bar - the barman took real pride in making great cocktails.
Both bar and restaurant were excellent value.

I cannot recommend Barefoot highly enough.
reviews.author: Matthew Gurney

reviews.text:
We went for the Radha Nagar sunset view. It was well worth it.
They take a 500/- per person cover charge redeemable at the bar but the drink menu is very limited.
Food could have been better.
We did not stay here so cannot attest for the rooms.
The ambience is wonderfully chill and the beach view is breathtaking. They give you tea and mats to see the sunset.
Absolutely beautiful experience.
reviews.author: Anjali Athaley

indeco-swamimalai.editorialSummary: Colourful rooms in a stately heritage hotel featuring 3 restaurants, a gym & outdoor activities.

reviews.text: An exquisite and immersive experience of South Indian culture in a picturesque setting, blending nature, vernacular architecture, history, and art. The food pallette offers delicious and unique local cuisine and some classic Western alternatives. The staff is highly professional, kind, and exceptionally dedicated. The hygiene is very high. INDeco Swamamalai is a place for inspiration and contemplation, enshrining stunning pieces of history, art, and living heritage. And to top all this, it has brought us together with amazing people, we now call our friends. Absolutely recommended - a must-experience-place!
reviews.author: Ivan Enev / Иван Енев

reviews.text:
This hotel is amazing. The hotel room is so big and spacious.The wood carvings are incredibly detailed. Food was amazing and the porri was amazing. There are many old items and Hindu god statues. This ain’t a 4 star hotel it’s a 5
Star hotel.10/10
reviews.author: Liverpool

reviews.text:
A magical place to stay and one not to be missed ! We ended up staying two nights, we loved it so mch.
A real museum, full of history and, everywhere you turn, so much beauty !
The rooms are boutique hotel style, with their own charm and character.
Everything is so clean, the staff very welcoming and the surroundings so peaceful.
The food at the restaurant was excellent and breakfast and dinner are served in beautiful outdoor seroundings.
A few nice few free extras such as a ride in a cart, a guided tour, a foot massage...
reviews.author: Phaedra Ravier

reviews.text:
Some days don’t just pass — they linger, like a beautiful echo in the soul. My day at Indeco was one such rare pause in time.

It wasn’t merely hospitality I experienced, but a warmth that felt deeply human — almost timeless. Every gesture carried grace, every moment held intention. It felt like stepping into a space where tradition doesn’t just exist, but breathes… where heritage and modernity don’t compete, but quietly coexist in perfect harmony.

There was poetry in the details — in the scriptures, the artifacts, the textures of stories preserved with reverence. Each corner whispered something ancient, something rare, something profoundly meaningful.

Guided by Brinda — truly a woman of extraordinary vision and elegance — and complemented by a culinary journey that celebrated the soul of Indian and Tamil Nadu cuisine in its purest, most refined form, the experience transcended the ordinary.

For a while, the noise of the world faded. Time slowed. And in that stillness, I found something we often forget to seek — peace.

Not just a day, but a feeling I will carry for a long time.
reviews.author: SR Fernandez

reviews.text: We had a memorable experience during our recent stay at INDeco property. A well maintained and lovely place. We loved the atmosphere. A great location and well preserved artefacts. House where Maha Periyava stayed is well preserved. Rooms are comfortable. Food was good. Coffee was a disappointment. Overall very very memorable experience!
reviews.author: Krishnamurthy Sridhar

reviews.text:
A hidden gem within 100kms from Ahmedabad, Easy to reach by Road.
Cooler than the City, the place welcomes you with singing birds all around.
Clean & Comfortable Rooms, Friendy Staff and selective but good food.
A must try ti break the stresful routine from city shore
reviews.author: Ankit Wadhwa

reviews.text: My family and I spent two lovely days at your Rann Riders property. Right from the reservation process to our entire stay, things worked with clockwork precision. Our rooms were large and well appointed. The service staff was exceptional in their kindness and caring. We greatly appreciated the fact that our driver got a place to stay and his meals were taken care of. The property is a peaceful haven and beautifully done up with lots of nooks to relax. I so wish I had known about the two decent sized pools to swim in - we would have brought our swim suits and taken advantage. . The meals were tasty and varied and freshly prepared.  I would highly recommend this place to others who might be interested in visiting the LRK.
reviews.author: N M

reviews.text:
Very bad experience at **Rann Riders, Dasada**.

Initially, they informed us that only vegetarian food is served, but after reaching there, we found that both veg and non-veg are cooked together. In fact, there was no proper breakfast available, and they didn’t even serve Hie tea.

It seems they are more interested in foreign tourists than local visitors. I stayed at Dasada Wings, and the room hygiene was extremely poor — there were lizards in the room, and it felt like I was sharing the space with them. Overall, ZERO hygiene — neither in the rooms nor in the food.

It was a complete waste of money. In this budget, one can easily stay in a 5-star property.⏯️

Also, the safari is extremely overpriced — ₹5500 + GST — and honestly, it’s not worth it at all.

Very bad experience at @rannriders_dasada Pehla evu keh che k only Veg che and tya jai ne pachi khabar pade che k veg non veg jode che, in fact aa rite breakfast to hato j nai, na to hie tea pan nathi aapi.... And e loko ne foreigner visitor ma intrest che... And Dasada wings ma j rokayelo hato and tya chipkali jode hu rahu chu k mara jode chipkali ej khabar nahi padi.... ZERO HYGIENE, naa room ma hygiene na to food ma very bad expo, waste of money, aa budget ma 5star property ma rokavay bhai....

Safari na etla costly price che k na pucho vaat 5500 r.s plus gst aa price ma safari karave che and sav faltu che...

reviews.text: Brilliant place! I have stayed here more than four times over the last 7–8 years. The rooms are very good, the service is reliable, and the food is decent. The property is well maintained and offers safari arrangements directly from the resort, which is very convenient. Located on the main road, it’s easily accessible. Overall, a very good place to stay and experience a bit of the Rann of Kutch.

spice-village-thekkady.editorialSummary: Rustic-chic quarters in a relaxed eco-resort with an outdoor pool, farm-to-table dining & safaris.

reviews.text:
Extremely Pleasant Stay, Highly Recommended

Had an extremely pleasant stay at Aliyar Village, Thekkady. The property offers a wonderful blend of comfort and nature.

The breakfast options were excellent, fresh and well-prepared. The location is very convenient, being quite close to the market and the Periyar River safari/boating point, making sightseeing very easy.

What we loved most was the natural feel inside the resort itself — it feels like a peaceful little nature retreat, perfect for relaxing and exploring within the property.

The staff was extremely courteous, helpful, and welcoming, making the overall experience even better.

A must-stay property if you are visiting Thekkady. Would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a comfortable and pleasant stay.
reviews.author: Amit Jhakal

reviews.text:
Had a wonderful stay at Spice Village, Thekkady. One important thing to know before booking is that the property is non-AC, but honestly, you really don’t need AC there as the climate is naturally cool and pleasant throughout the stay.

The property is beautifully designed with very well-built cottages and gives a true eco-tourism experience surrounded by nature. One of the best parts was the number of activities available for kids to learn about nature and wildlife. Their Tiger Club conducts many engaging and informative activities which are truly worth experiencing.

The food was absolutely delicious with a great variety of options available every day. They also organize cultural performances every evening, which adds a fantastic local touch to the overall experience.

The staff was extremely friendly, courteous, and always ensured we were comfortable throughout our stay. I had read a few reviews mentioning that the staff gives more attention to foreign guests, but I would honestly say that was not our experience at all. We were taken care of exceptionally well, and the hospitality was outstanding. Kudos to the entire team for maintaining such warm service standards.

Also, must try their Woods Bar and 50 Mile Restaurant during the stay. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a peaceful and authentic nature getaway in Thekkady.
reviews.author: Gaurav Taywade

reviews.text:
Had a wonderful stay at Spice Village. The arrival experience was smooth, and the staff was warm, courteous, and welcoming throughout our stay. Check-in was quick, and the room was clean, comfortable, and extremely well maintained. It's hard to believe that the property is over two decades old given how well it has been preserved.

While the food is priced slightly on the higher side, the overall experience more than makes up for it. The hospitality, service, and ambience were excellent. We stayed for just one night, but honestly, I wish we had planned an extra day here. A highly recommended property for anyone visiting Thekkady.
reviews.author: Ankit Jalan

reviews.text:
Spice Village (located along the Kottayam - Kumily Road in Thekkady) is a premier 5-star eco-resort managed by the acclaimed CGH Earth group. Holding an exceptional 4.7-star user rating, it is styled modeled after a traditional tribal village and is widely celebrated for its commitment to sustainable luxury.

* **Authentic Tribal Architecture:** The property features independent cottages crafted with elephant grass-thatched roofs, natural stone flooring, and coir furnishings. Reviewers love the intentional absence of televisions in the rooms, which allows for complete immersion in the sounds of the high ranges.
* **Immersive Botanical Landscape:** Designed to mirror a spice plantation, the entire village is woven with rare endemic trees, organic vegetable plots, and spice gardens. Guests frequently mention regular sightings of unique bird species, butterflies, and giant squirrels directly from their verandas.
* **Farm-to-Table Culinary Philosophy:** The on-site restaurants emphasize sustainability, serving dishes crafted largely with ingredients harvested from the resort's own organic gardens or sourced within a 50-mile radius. Reviewers highly recommend their traditional Kerala spice-infused curries and interactive cooking demonstrations.
* **Premium Eco-Amenities:** The resort features a large outdoor swimming pool, a traditional Ayurvedic wellness center, a badminton court, and an informative paper-making unit where guests can participate in upcycling activities.
* **Seamless Local Adventures:** Located just minutes away from the main Periyar National Park entry point, the resort’s naturalists and travel desk organize high-quality guided spice plantation walks, tiger safaris, and birdwatching treks.
reviews.author: GeoSense

reviews.text:
Spice Village is truly a must-visit destination. The place, ambience, layout, beautiful weather and warm hospitality were absolutely exceptional. The staff and the wide range of in-resort activities made our stay truly memorable. Among all the resorts we have visited so far, this was by far the best and we would certainly want to come back for more.

What stood out the most was how thoughtfully they catered to guests of all ages — both elders and kids were equally engaged, cared for, and made to feel special. Every detail reflected comfort, warmth, and genuine hospitality.

A heartfelt thank you to the entire Spice Village team. You all truly deserve a big shoutout for making every guest feel completely relaxed and for creating lifetime memories that we will always cherish.
reviews.author: Tina Thomas
